Today I spoke with Dan because of unfair practices from a bank. As I was explaining, he cut me off and then proceeded to not listen to the correction.
Here's the thing, the amount of money that I feel the bank is tring to charge (I'm alleging PenFed is engaged in these behaviors) is immaterial, it's the fact they are trying to  insist I pay  something that does not appear anywhere (ledger, statement).
I'm willing to bet if they are doing this to me, it's happening elsewhere, to others. Banks must be stopped, It's unscrupulous. It's the principle, and if Dan isn't  interested in looking into unscruplous practices, which others may also be experiencing, what good is the law?
I will look for a firm that may be interested in sending feelers out to find out if there's a pattern of consumer abuse. I hope my personal experience helps others.

So crazy: two weeks ago I realized that my teaching at OPS, 1993 - 1998, they never--OPS and OEA--acknowledged that I was TBI. This after telling them, end of year one: "The reason I have trouble with organizing is that I fell 3 stories as a union ironworker." One woman said, "I'm sorry to hear that, Ron." Meanwhile, rep Tom Scates said, "I don't know how much the union can represent you." Of course, I did get another two years yet not once did Dr. Mackiel or OEA acknowledge I needed accommodation. Year five, new principal, Deb Frison, threatened to put me on Formal Intervention again. Which would've made it four out of five years. I just told Larry Heck, I want to resign.
Bottom line, OPS/OEA dropped the ball big time.

Tom was great to work with.  Met with me for a consultation quickly and took the time to understand the situation.  Then helped me navigate it.   Always felt he was looking out for me and really wanted to do what was right.

I have had the pleasure of working with Tom, Lynn, and the entire office team for several months, and I want to take this moment to express my sincere gratitude for their dedication and unwavering support. From the very beginning, they have gone above and beyond to advocate for me, ensuring that justice was served for a situation beyond our control. Tom took the time to thoroughly explain every step of the process, making me feel supported and reassured that I was not facing this challenge alone—he treated my case as his own. Lynn was incredibly supportive, consistently keeping me informed with updates and ensuring I felt cared for throughout the process. From the moment I met Tom and Lynn, I have confidently recommended their services to my friends, knowing they are in the best hands. I am truly grateful for their hard work and dedication and wish them continued success as they continue to fight for others as passionately as they fought for me.

I highly recommend Fiedler Law. During a time when I felt overwhelmed and ready to give up, Kelly Brandon and her team were a beacon of hope. From our very first meeting, it was clear that I was in capable and caring hands. They were consistently prompt in their communication, thoroughly knowledgeable, and, above all, compassionate and kind throughout the entire process. Their support was invaluable during an incredibly stressful time, and I am grateful for their assistance.

Alexis and her team were wonderful- competent, knowledgable, and very kind. Unlike many other law firms, the team at Fiedler were easy to communicate with, quick to respond, and it definitely felt like they had my best interest in mind during the whole process. Highly recommend.

I was referred to Fiedler Law by another law professional and I was contacted within a couple hours of doing my web submission. Tom Freeman got me in quickly and instantly made me feel heard, in a time where I was at a very high-stress point. He was kind and knowledgeable, not mention resolved my issue with a better outcome than anticipated. I never was left wondering about an update, he personally updated me throughout and responded so quickly. I am thankful and appreciative and can't recommend his services enough!

His office is also warm and inviting- the front office staff was also friendly and responsive (and there is a precious friendly dog that will greet you, if you're into it).

I cannot thank Fiedler Law Firm enough for assisting me through a very difficult and emotional time.  Kelly, Kory, Lynn and all of the staff were kind, compassionate and professional!  I highly recommend this team and can say they are by far the best at what they do!  They achieve results and have their clients best interest at heart! It was a pleasure to work with you!  Thank you again!

My family and I were given Jessica Källström-Schreckengost's name by our trusted counselor. We needed legal assistance in obtaining a name change for our transgender daughter, and our other options were very cost prohibitive. After completing a simple application, Jessica most generously offered to take on our case pro bono!! She walked us through the process every step of the way, making sure that we understood exactly what was going to happen. She was always extremely patient, informative, and caring. Jessica helped us more than we could have ever imagined, and today our transgender daughter will forever be legally recognized by the name that she has identified with for years. A true dream come true!!! I cannot begin to express in words Jessica's level of dedication, knowledge, and passion for helping transgender people. We are forever grateful.

I’m not sure if I can express enough gratitude for the help from our lawyer, Jessica Källström-Schreckengost I changing our sons name. She was kind, patient, explained everything thoroughly. I felt prepared for the day down to where to park. I will never forget this day. Thank you, Jessica! You play a huge part & I will always send you all my positivity & love to you & your family!
Very helpful and friendly. Extremely knowledgeable and thorough. Highly recommended!
They were caring and professional.

Recommendation for Attorney Brent Nicholls

Attorney Brent Nicholls has successfully handled a variety of legal matters for me over the past eight years. I initially retained him to assist with forming a nonprofit organization, and he promptly secured IRC 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status from the IRS on an expedited basis.

He has demonstrated strong legal skills across a wide range of areas—from organizational matters to tax-related issues—and has also resolved lease and construction matters with efficiency and professionalism.

I highly recommend him without reservation for his expertise, reliability, and commitment to delivering results

reviews.text: My experience with the firm was very good. The staff was very pleasant and helpful.  When I had a problem and called Ms. Felicity it was solved within in a day or two. I was very happy with the results of my case and would recommend their services to my family and friends.
Was blessed to have him as my lawyer. But when I got my settlement  he seemed to brush me off. Kinda was hurtful.  With a little bit of rudeness. But he got the job done..

They wont do anything about your case they will just settle wouldnt recommend. That is incorrect you told me id have to pay for doctors appointments which i dont have funds for but its ok i talked to you 5 times in over a year i have records you guys dont care you just want your 33%
reviews.ownerResponse:
Marc,
We are sorry to see your review. We did not "just settle" your claim. In fact, you were advised that we were happy to file suit and litigate your claim if you did not want to settle the matter; however, the current medical evidence by your doctor's did not support that you would likely receive more compensation than the offer you had been given. You were advised that you would need to seek further medical treatment and those doctors would then have to give sound medical evidence that you in fact had more related injury than your current treating doctors were claiming. You elected to not seek further medical treatment and instead wanted to negotiate settlement of your claim based on the known medical opinions given by the current doctors involved in your case. Your claim is only as good as the evidence to support your complaints and unfortunately, you did not want to contest your doctor's opinion about the severity of your injury. Again, you were advised to file suit if you did not want to accept the settlement offer. You did not want to put in the effort and instead just wanted money to magically fall from the sky. The problem lies at your feet and not at our doorstep.

reviews.text: I highly recommend Felicity Minor if you need a fantastic workers comp attorney! I can't say enough good things about this woman! She actually returns phone calls, which is something that I've had a lot of trouble with in the past with other attorneys! I had a very difficult workers comp case, and Felicity Minor won the case for me! I honestly think that Felicity will give you the very best chance there is of winning your case!

Felicity has been my lawyer for almost 5 years now after a work accident that changed my life. She is an excellent lawyer.  The workers compensation process can be hard to understand.  Felicity and her office have helped me a great deal in understanding and being prepared for every step of the process.  I truly believe my life would have been much harder if I had not retained her services.

There are not enough words in the dictionary to describe this lawyer, Mrs.Miner. she is the epitome of what an attorney is supposed to be. I love her beyond words. I just sat back & let her take control & believe me, she did. My settlement is on its way & I've had to jump through every hoop known to man to prove to workman's comp t hat I even had a injury on the job. She guided & protected me from the word GO! Never have I ever thought I would say I love a lawyer lol but if you want to win your case & get as much as you can then Mrs.Miner is your woman. She was with me every step of the way. In fact I was shocked at the intelligence of the medical & attorney laws that this woman possesses, they should all have it but they don't.  Nothing gets past her & she doesn't let anyone intimidate her. I was so thankful a friend suggested her. I could type pages about her but it still wouldn't be enough to tell you of her awesomeness! Have you had an accident at work such as myself  & you don't know where to turn? There is no way this lawyer of mine could disappoint you in any way. You can't find better if you went to the ends of the earth and i mean that!! Love you Mrs.MINER!!!!!! She is as beautiful on the inside as she is on the outside. Don't let that little quiet voice kid you she is ferocious in a courtroom. I'm going to stop writing but I just have to say one other thing......Mrs. Miner is one class act , she doesn't have to act like one she is one. One hell of a lady!❤️

Not a good experience takes to long to receive mail from them don't know if you can trust the attorneys they tell you to say you are hurt and you really aren't settlement come in and they don't want to pay you I called spoke to the paralegal by the name of Sarah she  was a little rude when I asked when my check would be ready? she told me when Mr Miner is ready to give it to you. I don't recommend this law office to anyone for workers comp claims!!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Larry, it was believed that you were claiming to be hurt when you weren't and you didn't like that your attorney did not find you credible.  You were falsely claiming to be disabled and it was discovered that you were secretly working for another employer while treceiving temporary disability benefits for your injury that was so minor it resulted in no permanent disabilty.  You were rude every time you called our office from day one and treated everyone disrespectfully and we still tried to help you. You called demanding your settlement check that we had not received and when we did receive the check, we expedited your check to you and you received your check faster than is customary.  At no time did anyone tell you to claim false injuries, in fact you were attempting to claim you had more injuries to pursue and we doubted your credibility and we told you we were not interested in helping you further.  You were likely angry that we did not believe your B.S. and that is why you decided to leave a defamatory and false review.  You are one of the most deplorable persons we had the displeasure of representing.  Our firm represents people with legitimate injuries that are being wronged, not fakers that try to take advantage of others.

reviews.text: Frank help settle my dad's case earlier this year and I recommend him to anybody who has work related injuries that need to be settle by a professional lawyer.

What can I say about Frank? He goes for the "W" and doesn't settle for less.
He was in my corner for years. Hes not you atypical lawyer. Hes real and down to earth. He'll laugh, get angry and cry with you. Which is all the emotions you'll be going through with a work comp case. Frank cares about you and your well-being.  Theres no one else i wouldve wanted in my corner fighting my fight for me. I highly recommend him.

Frank is really too busy to pay attention to ur case. Even tho he got me close but ,33000 for not doing to much but a few phone calls is robbery in my eyes. He rarely made it easy on me. I ask for 75000 and to a hundred. Couldn't even get me close to but he made sure to make it sound like he was gonna do the most for you not even close I seen him two in a year and promise me to get them to reimburse me for my travels especially when he couldn't remind me that court date had gotten switched . All I'm saying is was him at your own risk
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Conn, I am surprised at your comments.  I represented you for your knee injury for over a year while you resided in another State outside of Nebraska.  My records show over 20 phone calls that I took the time to speak directly with you at length about your case.  I always took your calls or returned them timely.  Further, you seem to forget that I went to trial on your behalf.  As part of that process, I spent a great deal of time preparing the evidence and obtaining expert opinions from your doctors on your behalf.  As part of that process, I helped you find a new surgeon in the Kansas City area because you were unhappy with your then current surgeon.   After trial, the Defendant realized they had lost and wanted to negotiate settlement of your case and you agreed to accept a monetary settlement in lieu of having more surgeries.  After much negotiation with the Defendant, I successfully negotiated settlement that you agreed to for $99,500.00, not "33,000".  Unfortunately, you do not seem to understand or appreciate all of the time and effort that goes into successfully litigating a claim.  Even after you received your settlement, I spent months making the Defendant pay all of your past related medical expenses to your providers as part of your settlement.  I would point out that at no time did you ever voice dissatisfaction with me until this review and again, I am surprised by your comments.  If you did not want to settle your claim for $99,500.00, you certainly did not have to and no one forced you to settle.  Regardless of your comments, I do wish you well in the future.

reviews.text:
I came to Frank Miner as a Navy veteran, unsure about how any of this works and Mr. Miner was so kind, had described the process of everything and it’s not overdramatizing of me to say that signature on our agreement in April 2013 was a pivotal moment of my life...

To be 100% honest, Frank Miner is single handily the most skillful, knowledgeable, caring and compassionate man about his client’s and their family’s needs and if he’s able to help with anything, he WILL

My case was a unique one and it was so infuriating because the insurance company was SO BAD and delayed everything that they could it seems. Our first hearing was so nerve wracking but Frank was such an outstandingly skilled litigator in/out the court room setting and he was so prepared for every potential question that could be asked. He had literally made the other party look like they brought a toy gun to a battlefield with the knowledge and expertise he has.

We won our first trial, and everything Frank asked us to be awarded. We had to go through the appeals process and Frank was so patient as he explained the appeals process for me, (I asked a few questions so fast) and he is educated about my pain disease, CRPS, and he is the type of lawyer that just truly cares for his clients and it shows during times such as these...

Throughout the next few years, 2015-2018,
*another trial in 2018*.
Frank had every opportunity to stop  representing me due to all the issues we were having, and I know had a (in Omaha) SCS implanted and then removed a week and a half later, and I had to have it removed...

Deep depression and anxiety that followed and Frank was there through it all with me and was so wonderful to my husband and children, and he knew how deeply they were becoming affected.

Being a permanently disabled Navy Vet at 33 years old, is so hard to accept again, and I know this is a less than ideal situation, but, it would have been far worse than now.

CRPS-Complex Regional Pain Syndrome is nicknamed the suicide disease and at times, I needed a lawyer to fight for me and I never ever would be where I am today if I had any other person representing me.

THIS past weekend, Halloween 2020, he was negotiating my settlement agreement, and he was able to settle my lawsuit for an excellent and fair amount. I hadn’t even thought that day would come. Then, yesterday, I get a call from Frank and our hard copy settlement agreement that he needed my signature and notarized, and I was definitely in shock, again. Signing that agreement was the most surreal moment I’ve experienced and his paralegal also told me congratulations. Lol over the years I got to know everyone and this is a special Law Firm.
Thank you so much Frank for what you did for my family and I. I don’t have anything I can say that would convey my thoughts and my and my family’s feelings.
You’re an exceptionally amazing lawyer and I am unable to recommend Mr Miner as an attorney enough.
He will go far and above your expectations and his knowledge, experience, and fortitude to get you what you need and deserve.

If there were 10 or more stars on here Jamie Scholz, would be getting them .
She’s honest ,I fully trusted everything she advised me on , and after 7 years of going through ALOT , Jamie always had the answers and dealt with everything for me .
I would recommend her ,and I have to family and friends ,
No need to look further ,, JAMIES the one you need !
Thanks for giving me clarity in my troubles! You might say you people turn the lights on thank you very much and good job.
Jill responded to my inquiry promptly.  We were able to discuss how she might be able to assist in my future endeavors and communicated openly and honestly.
“An outstanding attorney! Professional, responsive, and truly dedicated to helping her clients. Jill explained everything clearly and made the entire process stress-free. Highly recommended!”

​I highly recommend Jill Jensen Law for anyone seeking dedicated and expert legal counsel. I worked primarily with Jill on a case, and the level of service, professionalism, and results exceeded my expectations.

​From our initial consultation, I felt immediately confident in their ability to handle my situation. Jill was incredibly thorough, clearly explaining the legal process and setting realistic expectations. They made a complex and often stressful situation feel manageable.

​What truly set this firm apart was their communication. I always felt informed about the progress of my case, and all my calls and emails were answered promptly. They were patient in addressing my numerous questions, ensuring I understood every document and every decision we made together.

I was directed to Jill by an attorney friend of mine and was pleased with the guidance that she offered.  She had a good grasp of the subject matter and gave me suggestions that will be very helpful as I move forward with my proposed civil action.  Thanks, Jill.

Jill is a treasure to work with! She answered all my questions, gave great advice and was a true professional in every sense of the word. She is my go to attorney, whom I trust and have confidence in.  I highly recommend Jill and her team to help you too!

I contacted Jill through her website and the very next day she called me! She not only helped me with my legal questions with starting a business but she also brought up topics I had not thought of yet. She is so personable, knowledgeable and I felt like she truly cared about the success and foundation of my business! Thank you Jill!!
I have worked with Jill for many years and she is very knowledgeable about healthcare law. She responds promptly and is very approachable. Highly recommend!

I will and would highly recommend Jill Jensen at Jensen Law to anyone looking for professionalism with estates, family trust planning.

Thank you Jill for the amazing advice and support.

Cheri
I called about a libel case. She took the time to explain to me what I needed to do,  even though she could not help.
Mrs. Jensen was awesome and worked very quickly in helping me get my LIMHP license resolved with the state of Nebraska. I would highly recommend her.
Jill has been helpful, responsive, kind and knowledgable. I would recommend her to a friend or family member!

I could not recommend Jill enough. She is organized, thorough, kind and tough.  I felt instant relief after beginning to work with her.  She will be in your corner and supporting you every step of the way.
Friendly service

From the reassurance at the very beginning, to the consistent updates regarding my case, to the fact he actually listened to me and what I thought my rights were and what I felt my damages were and exactly how my injury impacts my life. Aaron was incredible every step of the way. In the end......he secured the outcome I felt was fair. Thank you Aaron. Thank you for your persistence, your kindness, your compassion and most importantly, your wisdom.

Even worse, I was left mourning the loss of my dear husband & best friends whom were also in the crash, but were promoted to heaven. He was patient with my numerous questions, as I had zero experience in legal matters, and always explained the processes in a respectful, simplified, way that I could understand. He was very responsive, logical, and tenacious. At the same time, he consistently operated with compassion and integrity. Without hesitation, I’d recommend anyone looking for an attorney practicing workers’ compensation or personal injury law, to hire Aaron Brown to get the medical care and compensation they are owed.

Aaron Brown is a professional lawyer that cares about your case very much.  He will treat you with respect.  He handled my case with the utmost of professionalism. I looked for a lawyer after my car accident. Friends told me to try this one or that one. I don't know how I found Tabor Law, I'm sure it was Devine intervention. reviews.ownerResponse: T Lee, thank you for the kind words. Fighting insurance companies is the modern day equivalent of fighting giants. In the story about David and Goliath, David took 5 stones with him to do battle against a giant who clearly had a significant and powerful advantage.  We too need 5 important tools when defeating insurance companies who like Goliath have a significant advantage of size, power, and money.  To win our battles, we need: great clients, great evidence, a great understanding of the law, a great strategic plan, and precise execution of that plan. Through experience and hard work, we already have some of the tools we need to win.  We work with our clients to obtain the remaining stones, but we can't do any of it without a great client. reviews.text:
5 Stars—A True Advocate and Life Changer!

Having suffered a life-changing catastrophic injury, I was very lost. After struggling with a previous workers' compensation lawyer who was hard to communicate with and made me feel unheard, working with David Handley was a complete turnaround. Not only was he incredibly supportive and easy to work with, but he also helped me get the medical care I desperately needed, which my previous lawyer had failed to address. From our first meeting, David Handley took the time to listen to my concerns, answered all of my questions in detail, and explained the entire process in a way that made me feel informed and confident.

What truly set David and his team apart was their advocacy for my health and well-being. They helped me navigate the complicated process of getting the medical treatment I needed, something I wasn’t able to secure before. Thanks to them, I was able to receive the care that had been delayed for so long, which made a huge difference in my daily life. They were always present, professional, and communicative, ensuring I never felt left in the dark.

Before reaching out to David Handley, I had almost given up on having any meaningful future. It is a great relief to know now that the settlement David has secured on my behalf will help me for years to come while I decide what direction my new life will take. He has done his absolute best to get me every bit of compensation available to me for my ongoing medical care. I could not appreciate David Handley's time and efforts on my behalf more. Though I will always have to deal with my injuries and the limitations they place on me and my ability to interact with the world for the first time in years, it is far from hopeless.

If you’re looking for a workers' compensation lawyer who truly understands what you are going through, fights for your rights, and will go the extra mile to help you get the care and compensation you deserve, with my utmost sincerity, I can’t recommend David Handley enough. Their support has been invaluable, and I’m incredibly grateful for their expertise and dedication.

-Jessica Young
reviews.ownerResponse:
Jessica,
Thank you very much for such a nice review. I am glad that we were able to help you when another attorney was not able to give you the help, time, and attention your case needed and deserved. I've said it before and I'll probably say it again, great clients make all the difference when fighting with insurance companies. You did everything we asked and stuck it out despite all the gamesmanship, denials, and indifference shown to us by the other side. Thank you! We wish the best as you move on from this nightmarish past and press forward for a better tomorrow without the insurance company playing any more games.

Extremely Disappointed – Misleading Services and Disrespect Toward Military Clients

Update:
I was pleasantly surprised to receive a personal follow-up from one of the owners after my initial post. He reached out to offer a sincere apology regarding how my inquiry was handled. He kindly explained that he is a former military member himself and that the confusion stemmed from outdated or incorrect information listed on the Nebraska State Bar’s website. It was never their intention to turn anyone away unfairly, and he acknowledged that the referral provided was to a firm that unfortunately does not handle contractual disputes. I truly appreciate the professionalism, courtesy, and willingness to make things right — it speaks highly of his character and the firm's commitment to integrity.

Original Post:
I am deeply disappointed with Johnson Flodman Guenzel & Wasserburger LLP in Lincoln, Nebraska. Their website proudly advertises services in contractual law, yet when I reached out for help regarding a clear contractual issue, they outright refused to assist. Even worse, as a military member, I expected at least a basic level of respect and consideration — instead, I was turned away without so much as a genuine explanation.

Their public image does not match their actual behavior. If you are a military member, veteran, or someone who needs legal help based on what their website claims they offer, do not waste your time here. It’s incredibly disappointing to see a firm publicly advertise expertise and support, but in reality, show selective interest and dismiss those who serve.

False advertising and dismissiveness toward service members is unacceptable — and it deserves to be called out.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

A lot of us come to a law firm at one of the darkest points in our lives; a time of trauma, worry, concern, and uncertainty. Like many others, this was the state in which I came to Johnson Flodman Guenzel & Widger Law Firm, following a traumatic accident. Despite the state in which I came to them, I was lucky enough to be connected to Cameron Guenzel.

Throughout a three-year, arduous lawsuit that proved to have many litigation challenges, Cameron remained a beacon of surety and hope in my life. Cameron was always responsive to communication, and he was very timely with everything pertaining to my lawsuit. Cameron's demeanor, respectfulness, knowledge, and quiet confidence were always consistent reassurances to me as I went through one of the worst processes I have ever had the displeasure of experiencing.

I cannot overstate how grateful I am to Cameron and the team at Johnson Flodman Guenzel & Widger Law Firm for all they have done for me. I can say, from my own experience, if you are going through some dark days due to an injury lawsuit, Cameron and the team will be there to help guide you, reassuring you that there is a light at the end of that dark tunnel.

To Cameron and the team: thank you, all; sincerely.

My brother, his family and I were hit by a drunk driver in 2020. I suddenly found myself in a position I never dreamed I’d be in - needing a lawyer. Danny did such a phenomenal job representing us that he deserves a detailed review (see below). Here’s the short version -  If you have a negative opinion of lawyers, he’s the guy that will change your mind. He possesses all the qualities a good lawyer needs in order to get the payout you deserve while being patient, kind and humble. Danny gets results. In the end, I received money from all three insurances involved.

Danny is honest.
As I worked with Danny, I was continually impressed  with his skill, yes, but also his character. He is 100% genuine, honest and puts the clients needs first. He would offer his opinion, but in the end, our course of action was my choice.

Danny is patient, persistent and well-spoken.
My case was more complicated and drawn out because he had to deal in two states and with three insurance companies, but he never backed down. He stayed with me through the whole frustrating process always patiently explaining, in terms I could understand, what the other lawyers or medical companies were saying.

Danny is responsive.
I didn't have to wait days to get a response.  He was quick and efficient in his communication both on the phone and via text or email.

Danny is knowledgeable.
He has a great working knowledge of both the law and the medical terminology and processes. He was extremely helpful whenever I had questions.

If you’re looking for a lawyer you can trust and who gets results, I whole-heartedly recommend Danny Leavitt.
Great firm made my life easy, Danny took care of everything i needed kept up to date with everything.
Your support was excellent!!

reviews.text: Jason helped me with an eviction in Omaha. The timing and process were smooth and effective. Thank you!

The Hubbard Law Firm began our engagement with enthusiasm, but the follow-through on our collection efforts was extremely disappointing. I received inconsistent information, was told that certain people “no longer worked with the firm,” and my calls went unanswered while messages were never returned. Even after emailing Jason Hubbard directly, I received no call back—and to this day, I have never spoken with him.

Meanwhile, he collected his portion of the funds to his satisfaction yet we were never informed that our case was closed and the balance of our collection waived without our consent!  Whether our agreement allowed for this or not, the lack of communication left us feeling short-changed and completely sidelined as we were left in the dark!

If you want professionals who remain professional throughout the entire process, choose wisely. All we expected was clear, honest communication and a firm that answers or returns calls in a timely, professional manner. That did not happen here. They may be responsive in the beginning, but don’t be surprised if you’re met with silence and disappointment by the end.

Choose your collection and law firm carefully. I’m posting this to help others understand what they may be dealing with before starting the process of collecting your hard earned money!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Alan,

You hired our firm on a written contingent-fee retainer for a collection case after a tenant caused damage to your rental property. From the start, the agreement explained that (1) there is no guarantee that any money will be collected, and (2) the debt is assigned to the firm so that we can negotiate, compromise, and settle in our professional judgment, consistent with your interests.

In your case, a family member of the debtor ultimately came forward with a lump-sum payment on the condition that the entire debt be considered satisfied. That payment resulted in collection of the vast majority of the judgment amount, over 94% to be precise. If the debtor had filed bankruptcy, you would have recovered nothing.

Specifically, in your case, we obtained judgment in the amount of $13,515.00 and we were able to successfully collect $12,823.67 for you. That is over a 94% recovery. You received 60% of the amount collected as per the retainer agreement you read and signed. Our commission is 40%.

Our fees and the closing of the file were handled exactly as your signed retainer provides. You received itemized statements showing the filing fee, service costs, and payments, and our accounting reflects that you were paid your share. We did not “waive” your recovery for our own benefit or keep any funds that belonged to you.

This is not the first time we have explained this to you. We stand by the work that was done and the 94% result that was obtained for you.

— Jason Hubbard
Jason Hubbard Law PC LLO

Nash, Every day in the practice is a humbling experience. Every day I look across at a human being who is suffering, with family members suffering, scared and vulnerable and wondering how they ever came to be in a lawyer’s office in need of help. It is a daily reminder of how fortunate I am to sit on my side of the desk.

Then comes this review from a case a quarter century ago that leaves me speechless (saying a lot for an attorney). The reward of knowing a client carries with them the feeling of being protected and treated with respect is immeasurable. I am absolutely humbled by Nash’s note and commit to do my best for all clients. Thank you for this thunderbolt of a reminder, so many years later, of why I became an attorney.

I was on a depressing situation due to an incident of confronting the person that almost killed me in an almost car accident on W Broadway. The other party’s parents pressed charges of assault on me for blocking a phone's camera, which is ridiculous knowing the other person almost killed me and hit my car. Because of my situation as a casual employee and a student, I was depressed that I would have a bad record and would never be able to defend myself properly without a good legal counsel. I searched online for a good legal office that is suited to my situation, and I found Mr. Rater. I saw his profile and his reviews are highly recommended. I contacted him via email and he responded promptly. It was like an answered prayer for me. He let me talk to him on his office phone and told him what happened. He was willing to help me in any way he could. From that moment, I felt that Mr. Rater had a big heart and I found a savior. On my court day, he showed up and presented my case to the judge. My case was dismissed since my case was no record in the system. After the decision, me and Mr. Rater part ways. I was embarrassed that I couldn’t give much to him, but I will never forget his kindness and compassion. I appreciate his legal help very much and I highly recommend Mr. Rater. Steve Delaney represented the defendant in my divorce. Delaney continuously missed court deadlines which dragged the trial date out longer and longer. One of those missed deadlines was producing discovery. The discovery he finally sent over was one document that was nearly 900 pages long.

In the final divorce decree, which Delaney drafted, he also requested to draft the QDROs, which are documents sent to retirement plan companies telling the company how to split retirement accounts.
It has been nearly 7 months since my divorce was finalized, and Delaney has yet to produce correct QDROs. He seems to ignore follow up requests from my attorney.

I estimate the amount of funds that I am to receive from retirement accounts is over $200,000. And it's being held hostage due to the fact that Steve Delaney will not complete his self-requested duties.

Divorce is already a terrible experience and much of this has felt like legal abuse.

I write this review with great disappointment and frustration. I was referred to this firm by my first attorney who had to withdraw from my case because of a conflict if interest. While the attorney who worked with me at Reagan, Melton & Delaney was very nice, I only had contact with her about 3 times.  Each time we went to court for a 20 minute pre-trial she asked for a continuance. Several months later, about a week before my trial was to begin, a separate attorney from this firm called and left me a message that my lawyer was on vacation so he needed to call me and tell me she was withdrawing from my case to become a judge. No one from the firm ever provided any direction for me after that.  Finally I went to another firm that expedited the process and I was exonerated of charges.  When I tried to claim my bond I was told the firm of Reagn, Melton & Delaney had taken it and I would have to go there to collect.  I went to their office ( for the first time as I had never been asked to come there before even though I was going to trial in a week) and they gave me my bond less $5000 for their fees.  I explained my case and that I thought the fee was excessive for the minor work they had done and the people up front (one was a lawyer and one was office staff) gave me a blank stare and continued to talk amongst themselves while completely ignoring me.  I feel taken advantage of, ignored, and completely disrespected in an already difficult time. To others reading this please do a considerable amount of research before hiring an attorney.

I was referred to Douglas Novotny by a lawyer referral service in Omaha that helps tenants find legal assistance. At my initial meeting, it became clear that Mr. Novotny was unprepared, as he began by asking questions about my employer, unrelated to the tenant issues I was there to address. I presented him with a well-organized packet containing photographs and other evidence of clear legal violations by Cushman and Wakefield/Lund Company. He briefly glanced through it before informing me that he would need two hours at a rate of $250 per hour just to review the documents.

After only a few minutes, he ended our meeting to "go over" the materials. However, just a few hours later, he emailed me asking for specific photographs related to work orders that had been unresolved for over a year—photos that were already included in the packet I had given him. This showed me he hadn’t actually reviewed the documents but still intended to charge for the time.

Eventually, he suggested he could draft a letter to pressure the management company to complete the work orders but dismissed the more serious issues, like deceptive practices, retaliatory actions, and discrimination. He seemed to assume I was there for a worker's compensation issue and didn't recognize the specifics of my case or the appointment details. His only solution was a letterhead from his firm, which he claimed would solve the issue. I could write a letter myself; I sought his assistance for deeper legal violations.

In summary, he appeared uninterested in justice or the core issues of my case, focusing only on profit. If you're looking for assistance with tenant matters, I'd recommend finding someone who demonstrates professionalism and integrity. Mr. Novotny might be effective in cases like worker's compensation or car accidents, but he seems unprepared, dismissive, and without a strong moral compass.

reviews.text:
ZERO STARS...DONT DO IT...LOOK ELSEWHERE!!! He's horrible and ineffective. I should have known when I showed up at his office and he was in pajama/lounging pants and slippers. But I was hurt and desperate but quickly fired him. He will not talk to you (like he's that busy) all contact was via text with his paralegal. He is also  an ambulance chaser. He sent me a heartwarming letter after my accident, saying how he could help and he's there for me blah, blah, so I went with it. He told me where to go and this was my first time with this so I didn't know that he sent me to a chiropractor (that I'm sure he had a deal with) and I was instructed NOT to use my insurance (which I later changed at the advice of a real attorney) I knew something was wrong when the chiropractor's office didn't want me to do that. After several more red flags, (like him racking up hours for really no work) I know this because he was instructing me to do the footwork, I needed to call, fax, email etc...After firing him, he had the nerve to put a lien on my future settlement with a reputable attorney for his hours (laughable)! He should be splitting his fees with me since I did part of his work. But he probably billed for telling me to do the work!!! I swear don't use him and there is probably a statute of limitations on reporting him to the bar, so it's too late for me to file a formal complaint but I wish I had. Actually, I'm going to check on that. Once again BEWARE!!!!!!!!
*UPDATE no statute of limitations so I will be doing that today! And if he abused your trust, you should too!!!

Ms. Neary was immediately abrasive and argumentative. I wouldn't find it productive to bother to call this firm or Ms. Neary at all. I don’t think I said three sentences when attempting to answer her questions before she snapped back saying she wasn’t interested.  If you want to be treated like an equal human being, do not call. Kathleen/Cathleen was extremely condescending and rude from the moment the phone call started. They offer free consultations, but don’t preface with the fact that you’ll be judged, interrupted, and shut down before you even have a chance to finish your explanation. I found it strange that when she asked what I wanted to sue for was, and when I used the correct legal terms she stopped me and said “let’s stop using the legal terms”. What else would you as a lawyer prefer? On top of that, she began with telling me every lawyer would say the same thing to me, asked me more about my testimony, then changed it to “I won’t take your case. But I highly recommend talking to other lawyers”. Which one is it? The farthest thing from personable, reminded me of the employer I actually reached out to her for. One of the most unprofessional practices I’ve ever spoken to.

reviews.text:
I engaged Teresa Richards' services approximately three years ago, following several years of ongoing litigation. As a mother to seven total children, with two ex-husbands, each requiring co-parenting of two minor children, my case presented unique challenges. Despite my forthright nature and meticulous documentation, the substantial amount of litigation and filings over the years was considerable, resulting in immense stress and overwhelm. Throughout our collaboration, Miss Richards demonstrated exceptional professionalism, attentiveness, and thoroughness. She consistently responded promptly and sought to resolve matters amicably, prioritizing ethical conduct and restraint in contentious situations. Her commitment to doing what is right and her strong moral compass were evident throughout. With her expertise, I successfully gained legal and physical custody of my two minor children after enduring prolonged court battles, including two four-day trials.

Notably, prior to retaining her services, I received support from the Friendship Home of Lincoln and was able to exit an abusive marriage in 2019; however, temporary court orders at that time restricted my access to my children to every other weekend. Miss Richards' dedication, willingness to go above and beyond, and compassionate approach were instrumental in navigating the myriad challenges that myself and my children endured over the past years. Having worked with multiple attorneys previously, I can attest that Miss Richards' unique blend of professionalism and empathy is rare. Our collaborative approach during the trials was pivotal in achieving successful outcomes. I hold Miss Richards in high esteem and would recommend her to anyone seeking a moral, ethical, knowledgeable and diligent attorney who prioritizes resolving matters efficiently without unnecessary conflict. Through our experience, I have grown both personally and as a parent, largely due to Miss Richards' guidance, which has enabled healthier co-parenting dynamics, to the best of my own personal ability. Although she no longer represents me, our professional relationship has evolved into a lasting friendship, and I regularly seek her counsel and wisdom. Teresa Richards is a rare and exceptional professional, a superb attorney who genuinely cares about her clients, and when navigating divorce and custody cases, you need someone with her expertise and compassion.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Laura! I appreciate the opportunity to have been part of this journey with you.

reviews.text: For the first time in this entire process I truly felt listened to. I spoke with Amy and met with Megan. The attorneys took the time to hear my side of the story without interruption and without assumptions. There was no sugar coating, no bullshit, no pressure, just clear facts and honest guidance and realistic expectations. I appreciated how straightforward they were. They told me what I needed to hear not what I wanted to hear and that helped me make informed decisions. This gave me the confidence walking into the hearing this time around. I’m incredibly grateful  for how they represented me and for treating my experience as important. I would highly recommend this firm to anyone who wants attorneys that are honest, thorough and genuinely on your team.

Exceptional Legal Representation from Jon Braaten

I had a last-minute legal matter arise and contacted Jon Braaten with less than a week before my court date. Despite the tight timeline, Jon stepped in without hesitation and delivered outstanding results—successfully winning my case with only six days to prepare.

Throughout the process, Jon demonstrated exceptional professionalism, responsiveness, and dedication. He reviewed the details of my case with me multiple times over the span of five days and responded promptly to every email I sent. Jon’s work ethic is truly impressive—he is one of the hardest-working professionals I’ve encountered. He also showed flexibility in working with me financially, which I greatly appreciated. I can say without hesitation that he has earned my full confidence.

The week before she went back to her Mother's custody, our daughter asked to live with us full time and that's when the police callings and denied visits started immediately. Brody said we needed to wait until more happened to fight for modification and visits again, even though I sent pages of proof of what we were dealing with again.  Sept 5th, 2025 will mark 3 years since we have gotten to see her. We spent several thousands of dollars for joint custody for absolutely nothing.

Brody is absolutely amazing! For 6 1/2 years my husband tried desperately to be a part of his daughter's life on a regular basis and was never allowed to do so. We hired Brody six months ago and almost immediately we were granted joint custody and regular visitations! We have since went to trial where Brody fought hard for us and helped make our family whole again! If you are looking for an amazing attorney who will fight for your family, call Brody today as he's the man for the job!

Did a phenomenal job with my personal injury claim. Professional, knowledgeable, and very informative throughout the entire process. Brody and the whole firm helped me make the most of an otherwise bad situation. Answered every question I had and helped rectify the situation financially. Worth every penny.
reviews.ownerResponse: Caleb: thank you for sharing this. It was a pleasure working with you!

reviews.text: Brody has and continues to treat my wife and I like people he knows, not just as clients.  He is attentive, and responds to my questions in a timely manner whether it be by phone or email.  He has been professional, compassionate, and informative.   If I have to recommend an attorney to anyone it would be Brody, no questions asked.
reviews.ownerResponse: Tim, it is always a pleasure getting to work with the two of you. Thank you for all the kind words.

reviews.text: I’d highly recommend Brody for any legal separation or divorce issues. He took great care of me, understood my situation and gave me the advice I needed. Very friendly, knowledgable and made me feel comfortable with my decisions.
"Brody made a very difficult time for me, much less difficult."

reviews.text: Brody is a go-getter and makes sure he executes his knowledge and professionalism through and through!! He has gotten me out of one severe traffic violation and saved my CDL and got it dismissed !!! I highly recommend Brody as your next lawyer!!!

Brody has been an answer to our prayers,he has helped us for the last 2 1/2 years on our case without his knowledge, guidance and patience we would've never been able to get this far.He also has shown us the utmost respect even when he knew we were extremely frustrated, he always allows for our 1000 questions and never sugar coats terms for us .He's extremely professional and humble and we feel blessed to have him representing our side,we would not want to go to court with anyone else and would recommend him to anyone needing an attorney without a doubt he's fantastic!

reviews.text: Brody helped myself and my husband in two different cases. Both times he did an excellent job and was able to get us the results we wanted!!

Brody has been absolutely amazing to work with. I have always felt like I'm in good hands and he has always kept my best interest in mind. I dont know where I would be right now without him and would highly reccomend him to anyone in need of counsel.

Ok, no one likes going to a lawyer. They take the pain out.
Beautiful, clean & positive atmosphere.

Fortunately or unfortunately, depending on one's perspective, I've had the experience of interacting with various attorneys. Layf Carlson is one of the best. He was thorough, he followed up, he kept me posted throughout the process and did not settle for what may have appeared to others as inevitable. No judgement, he is an advocate for his client while having the knowledge and experience needed to execute!! I recommend him highly.

Anne is a considerate attorney with the best interests of her clients in mind. Her approach to estate planning was educational & helpful when deciding the right course of action for our family. Highly recommend!
Anne is a very pleasant person and makes you feel comfortable.

My son had Layf as his attorney.
He is an outstanding attorney. Extremely knowledgeable, and he works hard for his clients.
The outcome he was able to obtain for my son was awesome.
I definitely recommend Layf Carlson if you are ever in need of a highly competent attorney who will work extremely hard for you to get the very best outcome.
Thank you so very much Layf for everything you done for my son.  You are a true gem!!!
I've worked with a couple of attorneys here, and they've been first class in how they operate, would recommend to anyone.

Mr.Layft
I Highly recommend this Lawyer, He's  professional  in every sense, very intelligent, he knowns went to be tough, firm, and above all,thorough in all his work.He knows how to handle a different casse This is my second  winning case with him.

After what seemed like a great consultation, we exchanged a few emails with Kimberley and then unfortunately never heard back. I’ve been never been ghosted by a law firm but I guess there’s a first for everything?

It’s a shame because we were demoralized with our situation but our conversation with Kimberley gave us some hope. Huge letdown to be honest.

Unfortunately, we couldn’t recommend. Not sure if this is normal from this firm. We would have preferred a rejection if they didn’t want to take us on, but to just not respond and leave us hanging for weeks left a bad taste in our mouth.

Layf Carlson represented me in a leaving the scene of an accident citation. Layf pointed out all possible outcomes, and went to work getting the charges dismissed, which was the ultimate outcome of the this event.
He proved himself to be very knowledgeable of the law, and a very capable Lawyer, Whom I would use again and heartily recommend.

Working with Burnett Legal Group was the absolute worst "professional" service I've ever worked with. It took Burnett nearly a year to finish a relatively simple task. We had to continuously check back in to ask them to do the things they said they would, which dragged this process out. I wish I counted the number of hours wasted repeating myself. Their awful service was topped off with billing issues. Steer clear of this incompetent, apathetic firm.

Let me start by saying this legal stuff was never suppose to be my thing but due to unforeseen circumstances it fell to me. Mom set up her trust with Anne Burnett and she was great with her. After her death she and Zach Kneale were so helpful and very kind walking me through this process. I would highly recommend them. Especially if you have no clue what you're doing.

Charles “Charlie” Grimes is the attorney partner I had the pleasure of working with through Burnett Legal Group after a previous attorney dropped the ball on my case. The difference in working with Charlie was immediate and clear.

He was responsive, communicated well, and made sure I always knew where things stood. Whether by phone or email, I never had to chase him down for updates. He kept the process moving, explained the next steps clearly, and followed through on everything he said he would do.

Charlie came across as very knowledgeable. It was clear he understood the legal and procedural aspects of my case and his confidence in handling it helped me feel more at ease throughout the process.

My case involved collections on an out-of-state judgment related to a real estate failure-to-disclose case. Charlie handled it with professionalism and attention to detail. He was easy to work with, straightforward, and reliable throughout.

I highly recommend him.
Totally accurate & most direct route
Layf Carlson helped me through a very rough divorce. He was always honest, thorough, and professional. He stood up for me and got me a fair deal. I would not hesitate to use his services again.

My husband and I were referred to Joe Risko with Burnett legal group by a friend. Our personal injury case was a little more complicated than the average motor vehicle accident. Joe recognized this immediately and referred part of the claim to another attorney to handle the Workmen’s Comp. side. Joe continue to work on the personal injury part of our claim. Our case took longer than most cases to resolve. We ended up in mediation and with Joe ‘s expertise he guided us step-by-step and got us through a stressful but successful outcome. Joe is a wonderful attorney. He is personable, compassionate, and very knowledgeable about the law. We would definitely recommend Joe to family and friends.
Sincerely,
Tom and Laura Gaffney

Joe Risko led us through a long battle against a claim that was based on incorrect information, where we were NOT responsible for  the damage to another vehicle.  The case was ultimately dismissed, and the statute of limitations had passed so there was no way for the case to be retried at a later date.  THANK YOU JOE!

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
I can’t recommend Joe Risko and Burnett Legal Group highly enough! Joe represented me in a complicated car accident case involving a company vehicle. The accident happened four years ago and involved multiple vehicles, and the situation quickly became overwhelming when the insurance company for another party decided to sue us.
From the very beginning, Joe was an incredible advocate. He explained every step of the process in clear, understandable terms and was always approachable, friendly, and genuinely caring. He never made me feel intimidated or rushed and took the time to answer all my questions, no matter how small.
When we were unable to settle, Joe encouraged us to stand our ground because he believed in our case. Thanks to his expertise and dedication, the other party ultimately dismissed the case without prejudice—a huge relief!
Joe Risko is not only a skilled and knowledgeable attorney, but also someone who truly cares about his clients. If you need legal representation, I highly recommend Joe and the entire team at Burnett Legal Group. They made a stressful situation so much easier, and I’m incredibly grateful for their support!

We asked DeAna to assist us with updating our wills and implement an estate plan and trust.  During this process, DeAna took the time to explain and re-explain the different legal options that best suited us for our first trust. She was able to communicate in simple terms the pros and cons and helped us determine what was best for us by providing examples, based on our life situation.  Through her thoughtful guidance we learned to look at situations through a legal lense, which spared us potential dilemmas down the road.
We had a self-implemented time frame to accomplish our estate plan and DeAna took the initiative to make sure this happened.  She also explained that even though the paperwork was complete, that we have homework to do before the trust was truly implemented.  There was a lot to do, but it was worth it, knowing that the follow up work meant our family and assets would be protected and implemented according to our wishes.

Throughout the process we felt comfortable and confident with DeAna’s guidance.  She is professional, wicked smart about estate laws, and has a great sense of humor, which is important when dealing with such a personal and sensitive matter as death.  She also offered to continue to help us keep our documents up to date and assist our Personal Representative when the time comes. This made us feel well taken care of.
We happily recommend DeAna Shafter as your Estate Planning attorney.
Mike & Alisa James

DeAna and the rest of Burnett Legal Group were very helpful in the distribution of my mom’s estate. They were wise in the advice given, along with being encouraging, accurate and prompt in all correspondence.  I recommend them for the professional and personal care they give to their clients.

My mother used Anne Burnett for her estate planning. After her passing, this firm made my life so much easier. They handled everything and were so kind to me. Shout out to Zach Kneale for his expertise and patience. Highly recommend!

I worked with Layf Carlson and he is just the most professional and talented person I’ve gotten the pleasure to work with. He is always willing to help with any questions and just made my whole process such a time saver. He rocked!

Shayla is an outstanding trial lawyer. She has been leader in handling nurse home injury, abuse and neglect cases for many years.  Shayla is a past chair of the American Association for Justice Nursing Home Litigation Group and regularly teaches lawyers nationally about handling these and other complex cases.

It’s our priority to support families facing these complex cases and ensure that they receive the highest quality of legal representation. If you or anyone you know needs assistance, please don’t hesitate to reach out. We're here to help.

What a great experience...I can't believe how quickly they got us approved.  I think it was less than a month....

Grace Wiseman is FABULOUS!!!! I worked with Ms. Wiseman during a challenging contract negotiation for a leadership position at an academic institution within a large health system. She remained fully engaged throughout, bringing a thoughtful balance of creativity, discipline, and diligence to every step of the process and advocating for my best interests.

I felt personally drawn to this leadership position—largely because it would bring me closer to family. She maintained an objective, measured perspective that ensured my decisions were grounded in reason rather than emotion.

When the opposing side became uncooperative, Ms. Wiseman acted decisively, quickly, and appropriately by involving senior counsel within the health system/hospital to protect the integrity of the negotiation and to ensure that the opposing side could not find loopholes to opt out of the contract negotiation. She drafted a legal document to finalize the terms of negotiation that had been agreed upon. Ultimately both entities determined that we were not a good fit for each other.

Her professionalism, composure, compassion, and unwavering commitment made a profound difference. She was consistently prepared, responsive, and present, guiding me through each step with clarity and steadiness.  Ms. Wiseman is an effective communicator.

I also wanted to give a shoutout to her executive administrative assistant, Ms. Guderian, who helped to facilitate the introduction to Ms. Wiseman and assisted in our subsequent communications. She is truly a bright force that welcomes new clients and is always a pleasure to talk to when I call Horgan Law Firm.

I give my highest recommendation for Ms. Wiseman to anyone navigating high stakes negotiations. Her judgment, work ethic, innovative forward thinking, and strategic insight set her above the rest.

I had a very pleasant experience and felt a great peace-of-mind working with Horgan Law.  I had the firm on retainer for my business.  Fred handled most of my correspondence.  I was advised, I feel, in the best way I could have been and due to the advice I was given, averted any sort of litigation.  I believe that the advice and counsel was well worth the money.  I have since resolved my potential issue and no longer retain Horgan Law. However, if the need should arise in the future, I will feel confident and comfortable contacting them again!  I would highly recommend this law firm to anyone without hesitation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful review and recommendation. We are glad to hear that working with our team gave you peace of mind during a potentially stressful situation. Fred and the rest of our team strive to provide clear guidance that helps our clients make informed decisions and, whenever possible, avoid litigation.

We appreciate the trust you placed in Horgan Law while we assisted with your business matters. It is great to hear that everything has been successfully resolved. If you ever need guidance again in the future, we would be happy to help. Thank you again for your kind words and for recommending our firm.

reviews.text: My husband was in a serious motorcycle accident. I was referred to Horgan law firm and that was the best decision we have ever made. Not only did Tom take on our case, he believed in us and made sure that the communication was open. Tom and Grace both took the time on a Saturday to respond to emails, which is unheard of! Tom and his team truly wanted the best outcome possible for us. Not only is Tom a great litigator, but also an awesome negotiator. He was able to hammer out a settlement agreement for us that was fair and just. This was what we were asking for. Tom and his team have always gone the extra mile, making sure that we understood everything that was going, on every step of the way. My husband and I trust Tom and his team completely, knowing that they are fighting for us, and that we are not just another case. We will be forever be indebted to Tom and team. Horgan law firm will continue to represent us for whatever we need and I will refer Horgan Law Firm a million times over. They are a true asset to Omaha!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience with us. We are truly honored that you entrusted Tom and our team during such a difficult time in your lives. Your kind words mean the world to us, and we are so glad to hear that you felt supported, heard, and cared for every step of the way.

At Horgan Law Firm, we believe our clients deserve more than just legal representation—they deserve a team that fights for them while also being available and approachable. Knowing that you and your husband felt that commitment makes everything we do worthwhile.

reviews.text: I had a nasty accident a year ago. I had a brand new jeep I only had for 2 weeks….gone.  I had back surgery within that year. So now my back is in excruciating pain and my arm just isn’t right either. It was very difficult to get into another vehicle and my transportation was a must. I had a wedding coming up and was moving two weeks later I didn’t have the extra funds to accommodate this blow. All I was guilty of was not seeing the other driver run the red light fast enough. Jim was quick, empathetic, and stayed on top of my case. He made sure that I got every single last penny that I was able to get, and I got the maximum funds for my case. I was even able to get loans against my settlement to help accommodate my wedding and moving expenses. I am so happy I chose this firm and would recommend this firm to everyone that would ever find themselves in my shoes.
reviews.ownerResponse: Kimberly: Thank you for sharing your experience with our law firm. We are humbled that you took the time to recognize the hard work and attention to detail we put into our client's cases. We strive to be a source of stability and strength for our client's, whose lives have been turned upside down in an accident. We wish you all the best! ~Jim

reviews.text: Amazing law firm helped me in every way they could.

reviews.text: This guy is a serious jerk! Like most attorneys, (even retired ones) he thought it was okay to be arrogant to people like us that are simply asking for legal advice. Good thing he's retired, that's one less active douche-bag attorney around to look down on people that were unfortunate enough to cross paths with him.
He helped my husband and I with a legal issue and got it resolved without any problems. I would recommend him to anyone with legal issues.

The emotional impact of litigation can be overwhelming. In circumstances such as my wife and I encountered during efforts to construct a workable structure for everyone during an contentious custody battle it was an enormous relief to have Mr. Ferguson’s competent and understanding nature represent us. He not only prevailed in our case, but that success led to our being able to provide a decent, loving home.Jay Ferguson is an excellent attorney.
I, don't know who put this review of Jay Ferguson up ,In my name he is a excellent Attorney!! And a good person 😊!!

reviews.text: If there were more stars to give them, I definitely would!!! I was rear ended a few years ago and initially I wasn't going to get a lawyer. I started having medical issues from the car accident. My sister advised me to contact her lawyer, which in now my lawyer as well! I have called Greg for not only advise on this case but others. I work bankers' hours and he made himself available for me during the evenings and weekends. He was always patient, attentative, responsive, and inclusive to my concerns and needs. Somier was my paralegal and she stayed sharp and persistent.  I knew nothing about filing a lawsuit and they didn't make me feel unintelligent with my numerous questions. There were times I was having bad days, but Ricki, the receptionist and everyone else were patient. Chris Abooud was very kind as well. I hope I never have to do this again, but if I do, I know who I can TRUST! It was a pleasure working with them. I am very grateful! They fought for me and didn't back down!!!
