reviews.text: By far the biggest mistake we made was hiring Bill Bianco. He loves to bill for his time but gives sloppy legal advice, is disorganized and seems to have less understanding of the situation than his clients do. Trust all the reviews you're reading here. We wish we had.

If I could put negative stars I would! Both "featured" lawyers here are worse than a joke. They absolutely make promises they do not keep and will hand you over on a silver platter to the opposing side. My child is put further into danger because of their lack of common sense (let alone legal sense) to call the child's therapist for a letter the therapist wanted to write on the child's behalf. The list goes on. But they aren't worth the time. Save yourself the heartache and go somewhere else.

Bill Bianco of the law firm Bianco Stroh, LLC in Omaha is one of the worst lawyers I’ve ever encountered during 28 years of being an attorney. I referred a client to him as a courtesy and he spent months overbilling and insulting my client and his team while providing false information and failing to meet deadlines. He was rude, unprofessional, and wasted many hours of our time over a period of months. After my client fired him, I had a duty to report him to the Nebraska Counsel for Discipline to reprimand him for his unethical conduct. Stay away from this attorney and if he committed legal malpractice against you, report him to the Counsel for Discipline!

My husband hired Bill Bianco to represent him on a lawsuit he was filing against someone. During the time Bill represented him I have never seen my husband so stressed and anxious. He kept saying I think my own lawyer is working against me. Finally after months of Bill doing nothing my husband fired him. He has now found a great lawyer and is so much happier. Of course not before being lied too and mistreated. Please do not hire Bill Bianco!

Bill Bianco is the worst lawyer in Nebraska! I am going to try and keep this as short as possible, but considering all the incomprehensible things Bill Bianco did or didn't do with my case that won’t be easy. I have needed lawyers many times in my business career of 25+ years. In this time, I have had some pretty bad lawyers but none of them were nearly as horrible as Bill Bianco. I have had opposing lawyers treat me better than he did as my lawyer. After 4 months of malpractice, disrespect and ethical violations I fired him.

This review is the first of many things I have planned for him. Next I am reporting Bill to the Counsel for Discipline to be reprimanded and I am also suing him for my legal fees plus damages. From overcharging me for work he didn't do, to disrespecting myself, my experts and my other attorneys it was beyond bad. You would be better off representing yourself than hiring Bill Bianco. If you’ve had problems with him please reach out to me I may be able to help you. This guy should not be allowed to practice law ever again!!!

Bill Bianco is very knowledgeable and was extremely helpful when dealing with my mother's estate after she unexpectedly passed away.  Even though I live out of state, he was able to answer all questions and resolve any issues, and I appreciate the time he took to explain the various aspects of the process.

Absolutely corrupt. I was charged for many things he did not do or refused to communication. For exame to review a settlememt offer that he NEVER GAVE TO ME!!!
He refused to answer my questions but would answer questions I did not ask.
So many underhanded tactics to mislead. He cancelled a trial date without telling me, stole thousands from me AFTER I fired him and took weeks doing so.
Flippant mocking instead of advising on issues. I caught him in numerous lies from one paragraph to the next he would speak out of both sides of his mouth. He's dishonest, illegal and corrupt. He created a far bigger issue.

Bill is an amazing attorney. I should have gone to him with my divorce. With this recent series of unfounded allegations against me, Bill presented the facts which quickly dismissed what was being said against me. Everything worked in my favor and going forward Bill will be my go to. I highly recommend him and have nothing but positive things to say about his demeanor, representation and professionalism.
Great people, Bill is wonderful to work with.

If I could save people the time and the research and expensive  and the long term  damaged  it is worth making this post .(even if you have a lawyer insurance especially avoid this place ).. to someone that is supposed to represent you and defend me .. cost me even more damaged

Due to his late response or not responding at all most of the time.. I was made to look like a person that was not trying to cooperate in a timely manner respond to people involved with the case

Called  me one business day before my even first Court ..

Yes because it was an inconvenience court date that was given to me at no fault of  my own ..

And just because it was easier/convenient  to him to just drop the case one business day before court .. it was easy for him to say you can get a public defender ..

I want it my own lawyer and not someone working for the government my mistake and I am going to pay for For it ...

I just wanted to keep it clean record

And because of  allegations nothing ever physically nothing ever harmful  but just simply allegations
By simply he said she said ..
Case ...

Doesn’t even deserve that one star but Reasonable price. But very unprofessional when we were actually in court. Waste of money. Never showed any discoveries in court of everything I spent my money on as an attorney. Worst experience. Messed up on my case & trying to make me pay to rehire him to fix what he messed up on ( makes sense right? ) wouldn’t recommend even if he was the last attorney available in town. I guess you get what you pay for right 🤣 joke is on me
Bill helped us with our family Will and Testament for a very reasonable price. Has also been very helpful in the adoption case for my daughter. Need a great lawyer - Call Bill.

Bill helped us with legal work when we established Sign-O-vation over two years ago. I called today to ask a question figuring I would need to leave a message and hope to get a call back. I was able to talk to him immediately and got my question answered. I was very impressed.

My experience with Bill Bianco was minimally acceptable.

I never got the feeling he was fully committed to our case, in which my daughter was bit by a loose dog. On several occasions, I would ask direct questions via email and receive no answer. This is just completely unprofessional behavior.

During our settlement negotiations, the insurance adjuster asked if we had looked into a scar revision surgery. Bill forwarded the question over to us, at which time we scheduled a consultation with a plastic surgeon. The plastic surgeon claimed the surgery would cost 10K. The settlement was increased by 10K immediately. Why on earth didn't Bill have us do this before being asked by the insurance adjuster? Thankfully we were working with a pretty noble insurance adjuster. If it wasn't for the insurance adjuster, I am certain Bill wouldn't have had us look into scar revision surgery.

Unfortunately, the letter the doctor wrote was very unprofessional. It was a non-formatted single paragraph letter without a letterhead or a doctor's signature. In hindsight, I should have questioned it. Bill did send the letter back to the doctor for a signature, but he should have probably questioned the quality of the letter as well given its overall lack of information. This is what I would expect from any advisor charging THOUSANDS of dollars for his or her expertise.

The surgery quote was less than half of what it cost in reality. The surgery had no complications. There is no doubt in my mind the doctor just threw something together and never actually called the hospital and anesthesiologist to get actual quotes.

With the prices lawyers demand, I expect them to be thorough and expertly guide people through the process. While we did get through the process, I believe Bill Bianco probably cost my daughter at least 10K in her personal injury lawsuit.

I would never use Bill Bianco in the future, and would not recommend him.

I am giving him 2 stars because we did actually get some kind of resolution. I am sure I could have had a worse experience.

Bill has advised, consulted, and guided me on several legal and business issues. He has been ever the professional but not at the expense of being personal. From business establishment to divorce to business partner issues to IRS lobbying, he has, every time, steered me in the right direction. I'm a true believer in "you can pay me now or pay me later" to get the job done correctly. It's unwise to pay too much but just plain stupid to pay too little.
Mr. Bianco was very professional and helpful assisting my family and I through a difficult time. I would absolutely recommend his services!

Bianco Stroh is an outstanding law firm. I have personally known Bill for many years and have interacted with him professionally on numerous occasions. Bill is intelligent, well-learned, civic-minded and extremely compassionate. In my opinion, his biggest strengths come from his commitment to families, small businesses and the Omaha community. He  is also invested professionally, and contributes on a regular basis, to higher education, as a long-time educator in Business Law at Creighton University; and also has experience with international business law. When I think of Bianco Stroh, I think of competency and compassion. The Omaha community is fortunate to have this legal firm and Bill Bianco among its active business and civic leadership ranks.
Everything was perfect.  He took a lot of valuable time, and I felt comfortable with all the work I had to do and change.
The food was good, for sure! Customer Service needs to improve. I'll be returning to the Noodle Company again!

I have used Anne for personal legal help with our estate planning, real estate transactions and even some litigation from the passing of a family member.  I have had numerous customers use her for both personal and business purposes.  I have had nothing but positive feedback from those I have referred to her.   My family is very satisfied with all the legal work they have provided us.  Highly recommend meeting with her.
Anne helped my parents with their estate planning and was very organized, responsive was attentive to detail. Would highly recommend her for any future work.
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm glad I was able to help your family.  Developing relationships with clients is my favorite part of this job!

reviews.text: We had a horrible experience when we went to Anne Burnett for my parents' estate planning and Medicaid planning needs. Anne farmed off the case to an associate, Adam Wintz, who not only dropped the ball and required prodding, but repeatedly gave inaccurate advice about how to file for Medicaid coverage for my father. The firm's blatantly inaccurate advice cost my family thousands of dollars because my father's Medicaid claim was unnecessarily delayed and we had to pay out of pocket in the meantime. When we confronted the firm with their mistakes, they gaslighted us and tried to blame us for things going wrong. We consulted two other attorneys in Omaha to discuss the situation we wound up in, and both attorneys were appalled by how Carlson Burnett handled the case. I would never recommend this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Sheldon:  Attorney-client privilege prevents us from correcting the factual background of your post concerning work we did for you over a year ago, including whether your actions followed our advice.  We are very proud of our long track record of client satisfaction and favorable results.

reviews.text: Steve Schmidt did a great job handling Medicaid planning for my mom.  He walked me through everything step by step and was always easily accessible.  Went above and beyond for us.  Strongly recommend.  Thanks for everything Steve!
I appreciated Anne's friendly attitude and professionalism.  I would recommend them for anyone needing help with estate planning.

Carlson Burnett is a top notch law firm. Anne, Darren and their whole team are wonderful to work with; they always put their client's needs first. I have worked with them for 25+ years, and recommend them frequently to clients and family.

Anne is knowledgable, helpful and thought provoking when it comes to estate planning. My husband and I visited with Anne after we realized we hadn't updated our will in over eight years. Our will no longer represented our family needs, Anne corrected the will for our current laws and situation for our family. Anne is down to earth and friendly. We highly recommend her to all our friends.
Anne is a true professional.  Not only is she a genuinely kind and sincere person, she's exceptionally competent and trustworthy in the complicated area of estate planning.

reviews.text: In preparation for a 20th anniversary trip abroad with 3-children at varying life stages, we knew it was important to have an estate/trust in place.  What we didn't know is who we could trust to help us feel safe and knowledgable in getting there.  Julia makes working through hard/vulnerable questions like-"how do you want to make sure your kids have what they need if you pass?"--smooth and well attended.  She is truly an expert in what she does and her firm is professional, consistent, and reliable.  As we get ready to travel abroad, we feel confident knowing that our affairs are in order and that we can continue to rely upon her guidance as we update our documents in the future.  We would hands-down recommend them to any of our family or friends.  In every way, hats off...we are grateful.
Julia Palzer was a pleasure to work with.  She walked us through the whole process answering all of our questions in a calm and thoughtful manner.  The service she provided is worth every penny!

I had the pleasure of working with Julia for my estate planning needs, and I couldn't be more satisfied with the experience. She was always prompt in addressing my queries and concerns. One of Julia's standout qualities is her ability to break down the intricacies of estate planning in a manner that's easy to understand. She took the time to guide me through various options, ensuring that I could make informed decisions tailored to my individual needs. Communication throughout the process was seamless. I greatly appreciated the clarity and thoroughness of our discussions, which instilled confidence in the choices we made. The estate portfolio package I received at the end was impeccably organized and presented. I wholeheartedly recommend her services.

I have had estate planning at the top of my “to do” list for years and I just kept putting it off. Julia and her team made the process so easy in spite of our many businesses and busy schedules. Things are in order now and I am grateful to have this important planning task complete. Thank you Kellogg and Palzer!
Mrs. Palzer was extremely helpful in our estate planning. She explained everything very thoroughly and completely. We never felt rushed. So extremely thorough. Thank you so very much.

When we finally made the decision to set up a Living Trust it was an easy choice for me.  When searching online I found Kellogg and Palzer Law firm. When I saw Julia’s friendly smile on her website I knew she would be a nice/fun person and guide us through this process. Plus we share the same name! We made an appointment to meet Julia and begin the process. On our first visit she fully explained the difference between a Living Trust vs a Will and we left confident what we wanted and we hired Julia to help us with our Estate Planning. Julia is knowledgeable and a great communicator. She called and emailed everytime she said she would. We had documents ready for our review within a day. Absolutely a pleasure to work with. When documents where completed she hand delivered our Estate Planning binder to our home! Had I known how easy this was going to be we would have done this task sooner!

We found Julia Palzer from a friend’s recommendation.  We needed to do our end-of-life planning and had been putting it off. We were so impressed with Julia from the onset. She explained her costs and options in a pre-meeting phone call, and was so great at explaining the differences in a will and a trust when we met with her in person. We could contact her by phone, text, or email and she would always respond quickly. We now have a wonderful binder with all the documents necessary for end of life processes.  If you want a great lawyer to help you with a will or trust, Julia would be a great choice!  We are so happy we worked with her!
We had a great experience working with Julia. It was clear that she had been doing estate planning for a long time and was good at it. We highly recommend her. Thanks, Julia!

Julia Palzer is an amazing lawyer! She did a great job walking us through the estate planning process. She made it so easy and made us feel like we truly mattered. Everything was very clearly explained. She even found an issue with the deed to our home that needed remedied! WE HIGHLY RECOMMEND KELLOGG & PALZER!

My brother passed away about a year ago. I was appointed the personal rep for the estate. I had no idea what to do so along with mourning my brother I was stressed to do my job as rep. I contacted an attorney here local in Millard but after talking to him he seemed dissinterested. I then set up an appt with Kellogg & Palzer. When I talked to Jeff Kellogg I knew I had made my choice. It was a long process going through probate but Jeff explained it all as each requirement was met, It took about 7 mos but it is now over. Jeff was both great at being an attorney but also as being a human being. He understood that for me it was a big deal even though he had done this many times, His compassion for you made him the best attorney I have ever dealt with. I can't recommed him enough. If you have probate needs or a trust you need worked on contact Jeff. it will be one of your best choices

We had Julia Palzer complete our will and trust for us. Since we had never done anything like this we were very apprehensive about the whole process.  Julia sat down with us and went over everything step by step. Any questions we had and we had more than a few, she answered them in a clear precise manner that a lay person could understand.  When ever we texted her with another concern of ours she would quickly return the call and explain and answer any of our questions. Easy to talk to and very competent you will feel very safe and confident having Julia handle your will and trust process. We highly recommend her!

Stephanie Hupp was great to work with! She took over my family’s probate case from a previous attorney and we are so happy to have made that switch.

Stephanie was professional, communicated with us the whole time and handled our case in a timely manner. I can’t recommend her services enough.
Great legal team.

Do not hire Camplin. Do not let your lawyer hire her to do a thing. In my experience, she lied to me and lied to the court. Nothing is thorough with her. Seems to favor men - if you are a woman desperately seeking help from abuse, do not hire.
Very helpful and friendly
Expeditious, reasonable fees, friendly, knowledgeable. If I need legal services in the future, I will use McHenry Haszard again.
Excellent service.
Linsey was amazing! Got everything we wanted out of our adoption/custody case! Super friendly! Family oriented!

Linsey Camplin was able to help with our adoption. I had called several other firms who couldn't give me an idea, range, or ballpark for a simple step parent adoption. Finally, Christina told me what the exact cost would be. Everything was handled as expected. We never felt rushed or just another number. Our questions were always answered quickly.

Chris F. Blomenberg, I was lucky enough to be referred to, and am so grateful, honored by the the respectful, empathetic, and overwhelming professionalism, and highly skilled presentation and representation I received.    I was on a last chance appeal, all others had failed. I was emotionally, and cognitively so challenged, just managing me at times, was not easy I’m quite sure, but Chris was never rattled that I could tell anyway, as he calmly dealt with ever it was.  He was the same way with the judge who looked at me in crutches with a jaundiced eye, questioning Chris sternly if i was trying to pull something….Then I got to see him shine, and how much respect he’d earned from his colleagues: he chuckled, and assured him I just happened to have fallen on the ice earlier in the winter storm we were still going through essentially  It garnered empathy, even the opposing side softening after the explanation, and before we left the room I was confident he had earned every penny and then some- on what I lost hope Id survive.  He won the case, but more than that, he taught me a tremendous amount. He  also restored my faith in a profession that I had seen less than scrupulous actors as a part of what contributed to the disabilities severity, who he also counseled how might be ways to act towards those situations that served me much better.   Years after he finished with the case, I called him out of the blue in a panic as usual.  Chris remembered me and returned my call, and once again helped get me to the next step   I cannot speak more highly about him, and frankly, have met few people in my long life that have so impressed me in their person, as well as exemplifying mastery in his profession as well.   I’m usually ranting against people not doing the right thing— this guy, blows me away the other direction.   Not to mention a huge part of what at the last moment, fell into place, saving my life.  Yup, he’s a good one.

I can't say enough good things about Troy Bird and Amber Steffen at McHenry Haszard Law. They are the best in the business. Our businesses would not be where they are today without all of their legal counsel along the way. Troy Bird and Amber are extremely knowledgeable, helpful, responsive, and attentive to detail. They have always gone above and beyond for our business. If you are looking for a small business attorney, I highly recommend you speak with Troy and Amber at McHenry Haszard Law.

McHenry law does our legal work for my job. Troy is our attorney and he always goes above and beyond! I’ve personally enjoyed working with Troy while learning bits and pieces of law that come with the territory! He explains things if I don’t understand something, is very thorough, and overall works hard for his clients! 5 stars for this law firm!
Excellent legal services at a reasonable price
Reasonable prices and they try to keep the cost down.  In loving memory of John McHenry.
I would not recommend Shari Burkholder.
Sheri Burkholder needs to learn how to return emails.  Not impressed with her.  I see another review that she doesn't seem very professional.

As a fellow lawyer, I feel like I have a unique perspective on how lawyers should operate as a fellow Family Law (well, QDRO) and estate planning attorney. These folks exceeded every single expectation we might have had. My dad lives in Nebraska, far from anywhere I can be of help as a lawyer, so my sister picked out a law firm to handle his trust. These folks got the job done in the middle of a pandemic, have been nothing but responsive since, and to top it all off, the bill was even surprisingly low. reviews.text:
I don’t even know where to begin. My case dates back to 2017. I was in a horrible hit-and-run accident that was not my fault. The kids who hit me had stolen the car and were on leave from juvenile detention. This case should have been simple and straightforward.

Instead, it dragged on for a full four years due to the constant turnover of paralegals at the firm. I was passed from one paralegal to another, which created confusion and delays. Mr. Inkelaar did absolutely nothing to help—he only got frustrated with me and spoke to me in a condescending manner. He made comments about me being a “young girl” and told me, “this is a lot of money,” as if I didn’t understand the value of my own case and health. He got into multiple heated arguments with both me and my father.

One of the paralegals once told me that my case should result in a substantial settlement due to the ongoing medical care I would need. When we brought this up to Mr. Inkelaar, he dismissed it and said she should have never told us that. It was clear he was not properly overseeing his employees—or my case.

I wanted to take the case to court, but he fought me on it. He was adamant about avoiding trial and completely disregarded my wishes. I felt like I was fighting against my own lawyer instead of having someone fighting for me.

To this day, I regret not switching lawyers. I wasn’t treated fairly, and I did not receive the compensation I deserved, especially considering how clear-cut the case should have been. I later met with two other attorneys to discuss my experience, and both told me it was absolutely absurd. They agreed I should have gone to court and likely would have received significantly more.

I regret this experience every single day. Absolutely do NOT go to this law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: We sincerely apologize for your experience and appreciate your feedback, as it is crucial for us to improve our services. It’s clear that we did not meet your expectations, and we regret that you felt unsupported throughout the process. Your concerns regarding communication and case management have been noted, and we take them seriously. We value each client's experience and want to ensure that all clients feel heard and advocated for. Thank you for bringing these issues to our attention, and we wish you the best moving forward.
Thank you so much for the 5-star rating—we truly appreciate your support!

Prospective clients with a bad first experience usually have options to go somewhere that feels better. I had never hired an attorney before working with this firm, and unfortunately, it was an expensive lesson. I spent several thousand dollars for long phone calls and lengthy emails, only to end up with a simple state complaint filing—something I could have done myself from the start.

After the complaint was filed, I heard nothing for six months. The only follow-up was an email asking if I had heard back from the state (I hadn’t). Shortly after that, Goosmann informed me they would no longer represent me. That was it—no resolution, no progress, and no accountability.

Another attorney I consulted was shocked by how my case was handled and by what appeared to be unnecessary “padding” of my bill. In my opinion, this firm is more focused on billing hours than achieving results. Unless you have money to waste, look elsewhere for competent and ethical legal representation.

reviews.text: I had Joe Lonowski defending me on a tenant case. He was made aware that I was hit at a red light where I was stopped. The drunk driver hit me in the rear end and it ended up being his third DUI. I did not want Joe to take it because I didn't think he was qualified. He says he's got colleagues down the hall at the Poppe law firm that could help him if he has problems. I was afraid if I turned him down he would mess up my tenant case. He ended up messing that up anyway. I explained my injuries from the start. I've had to correct his letters multiple times to the doctors and if I remember right even the insurance agent. Dealing with PTSD and ADHD his lack of knowing how to do his job in my injuries created an enormous amount of stress. I could never get him on the phone without a hassle where he promised me that would never happen. Due to my frustration by his lack of handling my case properly he decided to withdraw. As of now the only repercussion he can get is for me to file a complaint. What is that process going to do about changing his behaviors. Nothing. But nothing is all I have now. And you're probably saying why didn't you fire him. It's not that easy to find another lawyer to take the case once a lawyer has started it. Plus he's going to get paid regardless of his quitting because of time invested filing fees what not. And me as a consumer can do nothing accept file a complaint. Really. In my opinion Joe Lonowski is the worst example of a lawyer. To me this man is so incompetent he shouldn't even make it as a clerk or a paralegal. I would advise staying clear of Joe Lonowski as your representation. Also don't let that rating that he has fool you either. Not sure how he even acquired that or he had people do it for him. Because he's nowhere close to being the rating he's at as an attorney. The other person that was in the accident with me has gotten paid back in November of 2024. He got me a little pathetic offer which I declined. I told Joe that won't even cover my medical bills. He advised me by saying well you probably won't have to pay them back. I said of course I'm going to have to pay them back. He said no not necessarily. There's a good chance it'll just be brushed under the carpet. That's the kind of representation you're going to get with Joe Lonowski.
Joel has been my family attorney for 20 years. My wife and I consider him to be of great character and he is commitment to actually helping his clients.

I used Joel Lonowski as an attorney in a recent matter. He did a great job making my case worthy. Joel took the time to explain things to me and made the answers understandable. I had several questions about my case that I did not understand. It has been my experience that some attorneys present things in a way of law that can be hard to understand. That was absolutely not the case with Joel.  There was a point at which Joel and his secretary had a misunderstanding about my paperwork and he got me right into his office to clear it up. My process was speedy and did not require a lot of my own time. I would have given Joel a 5* the only thing that brought that score down was that when I actually went to my hearing Joel could not be present (Which happens and he did send me a letter in advance notice and I was ok with it). The person who filled in for Joel was unable to answer a few questions I had that day. Joel did call me and clarify those questions. I did worry a little in the mean time. I would strongly recommend Joel Lonowski as an attorney who knows his business. He is a compassionate man! I did not feel like I was being judged for my situation!

Thank you Joel! You and your practice did a great job. Your help was appreciated.

Brian of Dominion Law has fought tirelessly to protect the rights of Landowners across multiple states. His work is always very professional and timely.  He is very experienced in the court room and is excellent in presenting complex issues in a way that everyone can understand.  I highly recommend Brian and the Dominion Law Firm.

I have been working with Domina Law Group for several years (now end of 2025) and will be continuing.  First (and on-going) need was as P.O.A. for my Dad as a High Pressure CO2 pipeline company included his Western Iowa farm as the place to cross the Soldier River.  Our currant LLC continues with the same need.    Brian Jorde is the specific attorney in the firm who has gathered one of the most united and extremely dedicated and focused teams of the most diverse group of people I have had the pleasure to work with.  In the continuing battle with the pipeline company a united spirit between land owners, family farmers and ranchers, environmentalists, activists, ag econimists, folks on all sides of the "climate change" arguement, and all from multiple states have not only held together over now multiple years in this fight, but have become genuinly supportive of one another under his guidance and leadership.  To read Mr. Jorde's legal work, to listen to him in his absolutely fearless, but always under control approach in the court room, and to be encouraged by his summaries of all that is taking place on multiple fronts at literally the same time, leaves me in awe.  I believe every "soldier" in this battle would echo they fight on because of the "general" we have at the helm.  The "other side" would really like him to go away.  I would recommend Domina Law Group, and Brian Jorde in particular, without a blink of hesitation.
The lawyers here are brilliant, wise, and compassionate. In other words, truly human. I’m very grateful for their service!
The rudest receptionist I have ever met. If they have him out front representing the law firm, I can only imagine what the rest of the people are like.
Everyone we dealt with was very prefessional and helped us out a ton. Gave us an option to resolve our issue.

I guess everyones situation is different, this attorney may have won a record setting verdict.

🤷 Maybe I would be a good attorney maybe even better than this firm.

I can even sue the UK if I wanted to. They attacked me in 2014 and almost killed me.

reviews.text:
I want to specifically recognize Tony Hernandez for the professionalism, knowledge, and dedication he showed throughout my custody matter. Family law cases are complex and emotionally charged, and Anthony took the time to clearly explain strategy, legal standards, and courtroom procedure in a way that helped me feel informed and prepared rather than overwhelmed.

What stood out most was his commitment to preparation. He didn’t just tell me what was happening — he made sure I understood why. That level of clarity and guidance made a tremendous difference in how I was able to approach my case.

Even outside of formal representation, he was willing to meet with me and help ensure I understood how to move forward effectively. That speaks volumes about his integrity and professionalism.

I am truly grateful for his guidance and would strongly recommend Tony Hernandez to anyone navigating a custody or family law matter.

reviews.text: I subscribed to my employer's MetLife law plan. From my understanding, it offers free consultations with law firms that are a part of this plan. I was connected with Johnson & Pekny, L.L.C.. I was paired with an amazing lawyer who unfortunately decided to take some time for herself.  I was then paired with another lawyer, Jaclyn Duffy. She was pleasant initially when we spoke. I had a conversation with her where I had a lot of thoughts going through my head, and I was sharing them with her. I was supposed to sign a document.  Due to the conversation I had with her she states she can no longer allow me to sign the documents even though I told her I was ok doing so. This resulted in a turbulent time in my life. Where I needed representation. She told me to contact her if another court date arrived. One did arrive in the mail and I contacted her as instructed.  I asked for her advice on the matter. She states to tell the truth and not say anything that will incriminate you.  At this point im confused and don't know what to do. The next day i have court i go in alone. The other party's lawyer approaches me and attempts to make a deal with me regarding the situation for which I retained Ms. Duffy for. In court alone being accused of terrible things with no representation. Since I didn't have any representation I couldn't defend myself. I lost the ability to see my child for the first time in my life. I contacted Ms. Duffy the next day to speak with her about what the day before.  I told her about the other lawyer approaching me to make custody arrangements. In exchange for agreeing to their civil suit. She only then tells me she doesn't represent me in that fashion. Confusing me even further. Im told ill just have to accept the outcome of the previous day. She tells me that she'll try to work out a custody agreement between the parties. Ms. Duffy and I discuss the times to be presented to the other side. After speaking with my ex. She sent me a screenshot of the times. That Ms. Duffy and i never discussed. I feel as if she was just trying to burn through my retainer.    By not communicating with me properly.  I'm confused dont know what I've spent over 2 grand on. From the billing notices it states communication. Which was always late and left me more confused.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry to hear about your negative experience. We are known for our client-centered service and would like to learn more about your situation and what happened. My name is Jerry, the managing partner. Please contact me to discuss your concerns.

reviews.text:
People hear horror stories about awful representations and have experienced frustration, even anger towards their attorneys. I have felt this way about my previous attorneys who represented me in court in the past. But this time, I was fortunate to have met and had an attorney who stood by me, listened to me, understood me, and had compassion for me. Did I win my legal case? No, I didn’t. In family and divorce litigations, no one wins. The parents lose, and the child(dren) involve loses. No one wins in a broken family.

But I want to highlight the service and representation I received from my attorney, Jamie Cooper. In my opinion and experience, I thought she was a phenomenal attorney! She is intelligent, knows the legal field well, understands the system, follows up, a quick thinker, and a rational attorney. I have made some suggestions during my legal battle. Jamie heard my voice, concerns, and frustrations. She understood where I was coming from. But instead of giving in to my every single request, she continued to give me wise counsel and led the proceedings the best she could for my good. Some of my observations about Jamie were that she has a good instinct about people. She spoke with our family therapist and used that encounter to guide our next step. It wasn’t what I wanted to hear, but I had to trust her instinct and counsel and believe she had my best interest. I am glad I did! She was wise with her instinct about her opponent- my ex’s attorney. That attorney was no match for my attorney, Jamie Cooper. She knows how to proceed with caution, aggressively, wisely, and rationally. One of the things I liked about Jamie was that she saw me as a person and a loving and good parent. She recognized my aching heart and my longing for my child, and my wanting peace with my ex. Jamie did not take advantage of me like other attorneys would, but I truly felt her sincerity representing me.

Attorneys are perceived as heartless and money/power greed. Many attorneys do not have a good reputation. In legal proceedings, you want a wise attorney to represent you and be your voice.  But clients also want an attorney that has a heart. An attorney that is still human who feels and cares. Emotional intelligence is also an important factor for me. Again, I was fortunate to have Jamie Cooper as my attorney, voice, and representative. She is more than five stars! Thank you for everything you’ve done for me, Jamie!

Mari Cris

Thank you for taking the time to leave an excellent rating. We appreciate it. Please let us know what we can do for you in the future.

reviews.text: If you want a law firm that over charges and doesn’t take ownership of your case- please give this crooked law firm a call. I had an attorney assigned by the courts as a GAL for a custody battle in March of 2019- the attorney and the entire office withheld billing information for the duration of the case and truly did the bare minimum. I was never even given a chance to pay them or discuss billing until almost a year after the case was settled. During this time I was left to assume the courts were paying the remainder or I assumed even the $1400 deposit was enough to cover the bare minimum effort shown by the attorney and her staff. They have failed to provide me a receipt of billable hours, they were not forthcoming with their overpriced services, and they have used their legal proceedings and court relationship as scare tactics into getting paid more rather than discussing the services which they billed and “didn’t get paid for.” Small claims court denied the case originally so they have now hired another law firm to send certified mailings with threats of garnishment. My four attempts to discuss with the billing manager Jenny ended in her stating they can’t speak to me even after agreeing to email me my information to no avail. I have yet to receive an email with my requested information. The information submitted by the attorney in the courts used my exact verbiage from an email I had sent her. Her court report was plagiarized, which at the time benefited my family but now years later I am in a financial battle with them demanding additional monies. They will most certainly take advantage of anyone they can- they are one of the top 5 most hired attorneys office from Douglas county court if that doesn’t say it all. I would not recommend this attorneys office to anyone. I can go on but hopefully you get the point. I’m a huge advocate for the legal process and proceedings allotted to us as humans but when you get someone supposed to be on your side but appointed by the courts- you should know they are going to be deceitful, probably in it for the wrong reasons, and they’ll win because they do it all the time. Lesson learned the hard way.

Thankfully, I was referred to Sandra Stern by a friend.

Sandra was extremely professional, knowledgeable, and straightforward. She was honest about all potential outcomes. She listened to me and understood exactly what help I needed.  She remained focused on winning my case and not simply racking up a bill. Throughout the entire process, Sandra represented me aggressively and proactively.  She was responsive and it was easy to reach her whenever I had questions or concerns. She always let me know what to expect and was on top of every development.

Sandra knew all parties involved in my case and they spoke highly of her. It was very clear to me that she is a well-known and respected attorney.  She was worth every penny and then some.  I am very grateful for her because my case could have gone the other way very easily.

I have recommended her to others and will continue to do so.  She is trustworthy and will let you know whether she can help you or not; she is not one of those attorneys who would just take your money regardless. While I hope that I do not need an attorney again any time soon, Sandra Stern would be my first call if I did. 5/5.  Thank you Sandra!

reviews.text:
Dave is hands down THE Nebraska tenant–landlord attorney you want in your corner.

I came to Dave incredibly overwhelmed and exhausted after ongoing issues in my rental unit and building. Dave stepped in immediately, explained my rights under Nebraska’s 14/30 notice law and took over every stressful communication on my behalf. He was clear, compassionate, and steady — the exact combination of hope you need when things as important as this feel hopeless.
He assisted me in drafting a 14/30 notice and ended up negotiating a transfer into a safe new apartment, while fully protecting my rights. He also made sure I understood every option (including potential compensation and timelines) and never made me feel rushed or lost in legal jargon. He responded quickly and kept me calm, informed, and up to date the entire way. That level of care says everything.

Smart, compassionate, and no fluff — just results. If you need a skilled Nebraska tenant advocate who also connects on a deeply human level, Dave is it. 100% recommend.

I hired Whelan Law Office for a custody case, and I can honestly say it was one of the biggest mistakes I’ve ever made.

The initial consultation was a masterclass in false confidence. They promised a well-organized, aggressive strategy and made me feel like I was in the best hands possible. I paid a significant retainer fee, believing that their professionalism would pay off.

The reality, however, was a complete betrayal of that initial trust. When we got to court, it was like watching a completely different firm at work. My lawyer seemed unprepared and lacked the fight they had promised. They essentially laid down and allowed the opposing counsel to run over our case with little to no resistance. All the promises of a strong defense and a meticulous strategy dissolved into a passive, lackluster performance.

My money was spent, my time was wasted, and the outcome was devastating. If you're looking for a law office that will actually fight for you, I'd suggest you keep looking. This firm is all talk and no action. I truly regret trusting them with something as important as my family's future.
Mr. Whelan and his team were 100% exactly what I prayed for. They were precise, courteous and professional throughout my divorce. Very detail oriented and meticulous!!! Also very personable.

reviews.ownerResponse: We're sorry you feel this way.  Last year we promptly responded to your inquiry about possible representation and requested that you send us information and supporting documents so we could evaluate your potential case.  We didn't receive anything from you until 4 months later.  The little information you sent us was insufficient so we had to request more information and also schedule an interview with you.  After we finally had sufficient information we reviewed it and informed you that we would be unable to represent you less than two weeks later.  We also invited you to reach back out if your circumstances changed in the future.  We receive many inquiries and carefully review all of them, but unfortunately cannot possibly accept every case.  We appreciate the understanding of our clients and potential clients who contact us.

reviews.text: I was impressed with Michael Merrick at Merrick Law Firm. Mike’s level of professionalism and expertise was apparent from the very beginning, and I felt confident that I was in capable hands.  Mike was always available to answer questions or concerns I had. He kept me updated on the progress of my case every step of the way. He put me at ease throughout the legal process. He has a thorough knowledge of the law and defended my case with determination. Mike did an excellent job in the representation of my employment case.  I would highly recommend Michael Merrick at Merrick Law Firm in Omaha, Nebraska to handle your employment case. I sincerely thank you Mike for the outstanding job!
Highly recommend Bonnie!’ Amazing!!
They never call back.
reviews.ownerResponse: Nolan, we checked our phone system and do not see a voicemail from anyone with your name.  We would be happy to take your call if you try us at (402) 951-9330.

reviews.text: My experience with Mr. Merrick handling my case, was exceptional.  I never needed legal support previously, and Mr. Merrick was very responsive and provided sound and logical advice.  I would recommend Mr. Merrick without hesitation, his integrity and professionalism were exactly what I was looking for and needed to achieve a reasonable outcome.
5 star representation! Knowledge of the trade is unmatched.  Highly recommend Merrick Law.

I highly recommend Merrick Law Firm LLC.

reviews.ownerResponse: Our office staff members manage multiple phone lines and receive many calls often at the same time which may require placing a caller on a brief hold.  We regret any inconvenience this may cause and greatly appreciate the patience and understanding of our loyal clients and others who call seeking our assistance.

reviews.text: From the moment I called the law firm to ask questions about concerns I was going through and dealing with, I was quickly at ease. My questions were answered, and they followed up with questions and information they gathered very quickly. At each step, I was kept informed of what was happening and what the next options were. Mr. Merrick took the time to listen to my input and I felt he valued it and added to what he compiled. It was done and over and it went very smoothly for me. He clearly knows the law, the States and the Courts, what can be accomplished and what cannot, and at the heart of it, he had my best interests as his focus and concern. I was very pleased with the outcome.

Working with Michael Merrick was a great experience. I am forever grateful he took my case and believed in me. He is very professional, knowledgeable, compassionate and assertive. While working with him, I grew to admire and respect him and his opinion very much. I could not have been in better hands.

After going through one of the worst experiences of my life with a employer, I was lucky enough for him to believe in me and what I had been through. I never once felt he didn’t have my best interest at heart.
Honestly, I don’t know how I would have ever gotten through the whole process without him and his expertise, knowledge and guidance. I never once felt I was all alone through any of it , he was always there for me and was just a phone call away.
I had very little faith in our legal system prior to going through this. Working with Michael Merrick, has proven to me having the BEST to represent you is key to having your case and what you went through be heard.

reviews.ownerResponse: We have no record of you as a client or that you ever contacted our office. If you really have a concern please call our office so we can address it.  Thank you.

reviews.text: Mike was easy to work with and very helpful. I had no clue on the next steps and he helped me every step of the way and took the stress off of me.

Mike was honest, understanding, and very reliable for me! He made everything easy, understandable and helped me in every way he could! I’d highly recommend him to have as a lawyer! Super friendly, and an all around genuinely great guy! If I could put 10 stars I would!

Mike takes the time and does his research. You couldnt ask for a better employment attorney. HIGHLY recomend Merrick Law firm!

My experience with Merrick Law Firm has been nothing but professional and exceptional.  Mike is extremely hard working and dedicated to his clients. His many years of experience lend to his unmatched knowledge and expertise in all areas of employment law. Thanks!
Preston was very active during my trial. He answered all my questions and helped me very much understand the process.
We achieved the perfect outcome for my specific situation. I am glad this is the lawyer I hired.

I feel like I owe him a fee but since my situation did not require a retention of service he didn't want one. I would recommend him for anyone that needs help in Nebraska. Thank you sir!
Weekend call and still called me back and lead me to resources to help me!

Was told he was not interested in that!!!! Don't even call these people...

Casey J. Quinn has had many happy customers. I'm his customer right now and we'll see how this goes after 4 months and then I will make an assessment on March 13th. Then & only will I see about making a review. Well, I'm back to finish my review on March 15th, 2025. I had Casey for 3 months & I will admit that at first, I was not impressed with his ethics. For an attorney with 40 years of experience, I figured that he should be like " Matlock or Perry Mason ". Unfortunately, I expected more from him. Considering I have a Mental disability, I don't know exactly what I'm doing in life because of my head injury, but everyone kept blaming the disabled person. Anyway, I still say that Casey could've done much more for me. At the end I ended up with a misdemeanor & probation for 1 year. He didn't do what I wanted, but he did do what he could and for that Casey, I truly thank you. I wish I had more money to give you, but I don't and for that I'm sorry. It's now May 24th, 2025 & Casey Quinn has treated me like a leper. He's been lying to me since he found out about a conviction almost 40 years ago and has used that against me ever since. I'm the victim here and he treats me like a common criminal. The Judge asked me if my attorney is on the up and up and I kept my mouth shut. I should have told him that I was not happy with my attorney. Then I got probation with a P.O. who treats me like a Criminal and Casey won't do anything for me.
Casey is so amazing I would recommend him to anyone and I would not want anyone else to represent me besides him!!! I will forever be greatful for that man
Thank you for helping my mother in  law figure things with my significant other! These charges will be dropped thank you so much

Justin Quinn has handled my affairs for over 10 years now. Since day one he has been 100% hands on, fully involved and addressing concern when it's needed and providing me updates throughout the process. During one situation in my life he was unable to handle so he referred me to another competent lawyer who in fact won my case. Justin is top notch lawyer and extreamly involved getting every detail with understanding your needs/wants. He is blunt and straight to the point. Justin will continually have my business! Thanks!

Justin Quinn is phenomenal.  He is realistic, fair, and can help you stay balanced through some of the most stressful times in your life!  You want him on your team.  He will see things through to the end and is reliable.  I can't say enough about his dedication to his clients.  Thank you Justin!!!

Chris and his team at Vacanti Shattuck Finocchiaro were excellent to work with.  Very responsive, collaborative, and efficient working through a challenging situation.  There was never a moment where I felt like Chris didn't have my best interests at heart.

Matt

Chris, Will, and Suzy were great to work with during my divorce. It was obviously a difficult time, but they made the process much easier by being professional, responsive, and genuinely supportive. They kept me informed, answered my questions, and made sure I always understood what was going on. I truly felt like they had my best interests in mind the entire time. I’m very grateful for their help and would absolutely recommend them to anyone going through a divorce.
Chris and Team helped me get though a tough time in my life.  They helped me every step of the way and looked out for my children's and my best interest.

Chris Vacanti and his paralegal, Suzy, were compassionate and friendly guides through a difficult time.  In the midst of multiple roadblocks and an array of uncertainties, they provided much-needed stability and hope.  Chris is a skilled, articulate, and tough attorney who did everything possible to achieve the most ideal outcome for me as his client.  He understands the value and necessity of compassion and compromise.  When it comes to responsiveness, his level of customer service is unmatched.  Any request I’ve made for assistance, update, or feedback has been answered in an uncommonly timely manner.  Chris had my utmost respect from our very first conversation, and all of our interactions through the years have continued to bolster my regard for him.

Kelly Shattuck has been my attorney for a custody case. He is straight forward and answers questions clearly and quickly. I highly recommend him and anyone else in the firm. They'll definitely get the job done!

I had a fantastic experience with this law office. The attorneys are incredibly knowledgeable and made a real difference in my case. Kelly was a real game changer in the courtroom. He was able to get a clear picture across to the Judge in my case and defended me wonderfully. I’m so grateful for their help and would definitely use them again!
The firm does fabulous work.  Their creativity and attention to detail stands out.  Kelly was very accessible throughout our work together.

Kelly Shattuck did an amazing job with my custody issues. He shows up to court more than prepared and definitely knows what he’s doing. Kelly has compassion and is very easy to talk to. His legal assistant is also amazing and she will go above and beyond to help with any questions. Highly recommended.
Chris Vacanti is incredible - would highly recommend. He is extremely thorough and legitimately cares about what's best for his clients. His personal approach sets him apart from other Attorneys.

I highly recommend Kelly Shattuck for anyone needing assistance with a divorce. Going through that process can be overwhelming and emotional, but Kelly made everything so much easier to understand. He has a wonderful way of explaining things clearly without making it feel intimidating or complicated.

Kelly’s light-heartedness and calm personality truly helped ease my stress during a difficult time. He was professional, supportive, and always made me feel comfortable asking questions. I’m very grateful for his guidance and would absolutely recommend him to anyone needing legal help.

I have been represented by Chris Vacanti for more than a decade regarding my custody and child support situation, and I cannot recommend him highly enough. Chris does more than just provide legal counsel; he 'calms the waters' in situations that often feel overwhelming.

What sets Chris and his firm apart is their genuine care. I have always felt that Chris treats my case and provides guidance with the same care and attention he would for his own family. Most importantly, he prioritizes the well-being of my son above all else. In my experience, many attorneys tend to escalate tense situations to generate more revenue, but Chris is the exact opposite, he works to de-escalate conflict and find the best path forward. For me, this hasn't felt like a business transaction; it’s been a decade-long relationship built on trust and results

I’ve had the opportunity to work with this law firm and also receive legal assistance from them, and the experience was exceptional. Chris Vacanti is highly professional, compassionate, and extremely dedicated to his clients. The level of care and commitment provided made a meaningful difference, and I would highly recommend this firm.
100% satisfied. Mr. Vacanti is professional. Kept me at peace in process and resolved situation quickly and got me what was promised. Thank you very much!
Grace is an amazing lawyer ! She worked her butt off to get my case dropped !! Highly recommend it 10/10! She’s so sweet and kind and actually listens !!
Grace was amazing with helping our family! Goes above and beyond for her clients!!!!
Grace Boothe-Hanna has done a great job assisting my parents with their will. Timely and professional. She's become the family lawyer now. We are thankful for the work she has put in for us.

We are planning a trip that would take us into parts of Canada and we had some legal question related to our trip. We weren't sure where to start so we contacted a lawyer we had used in the past, Dave Mitchell. After reviewing our request, he referred us to another lawyer in the firm, Grace C. Boothe-Hanna, that specializes in the area we needed help with. We contacted Grace, explained our needs, and she said she would research the issue and get back to us, which she did promptly. She was wonderful to work with. She was timely, kept us in the loop of where she was at on the issue, professional and understanding of our situation. She was also able to refer us to a Canadian lawyer so we could get a local Canadian perspective on the situation along with hers. I would definitely use Attorney Grace Boothe-Hanna and the Yost, Lamme, Hillis, Mitchell, Schulz,
Hartmann, Wilson, & Borgmann, P.C. law firm again.

Yost law firm is handling a hernia mesh that was put in some time ago I'm so grateful for there support in this matter  it's been a long process 2 get to this addressed the staff has been so supportive emotionally and providing expertise   guideness in this matter there is still a long road ahead yet   I have complete confidence in Yost law firm and staffing I had spoken to 25 plus law plus law firms and Yost law firm was the only firm willing to step up to the plate and help address the pharmaceutical company for this implant  if  there is any one else dealing with this kind of matter please would love to hear from you  as there are definitely obstacles ahead well Mr Yost thank you so much and your staff sincerely perry miller

I recently met on the phone with Jack Ruesch for a civil legal issue. I have spent weeks compiling data and talking with many relevant personnel. I fully respect and understand an attorney's right to take on (or not take on) cases at their discretion, but I am severely disappointed in Jack.

He declined to meet with me any further on the issue and refused to even review the data I had compiled. In my brief interaction with him, I came away with the opinion that he is a lazy old man who's mailing it in at the end of his career.

As a result, I do not recommend approaching him with any issue that requires any actual legal effort. Sometimes, lawyers should do things because it's the right thing to do. I don't believe Jack is the guy to meet that expectation.

To his credit, he did provide me with a referral to another attorney who was far more interested in providing guidance and proper guidance.

This is my opinion based on my experience.
Absolutely no help from the receptionist... i would think that a lawfirm could havd at least pointed me in the right direction
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Nicole, we are so sorry you had that experience when calling us. I invite you to please call back, ask for Nicole Hughes, and I will be glad to personally see if I can help you or at least help you go in the right direction.

reviews.text: Not only did I not get a call back, I didn't get the courtesy of an apology.  John should be embarrassed.  The firm should be abhorred. Obviously the below reviews are from friends, aquaintences, employees.  Sad

He also was very good about explaining the options available to me and sharing his expertise on the best way to collect on past due accounts.   With Ryan's help, the past due balances have been recovered.  I will be using Ryan again in the future.

I had a great experience with Telpner Peterson Law Firm. I highly recommend getting in contact with Ryan Muldoon! He was my attorney and did a fantastic job! He would call me periodically to check up on me and inform me on the status of my case. Couldn't have asked for a better experience.

Good afternoon, first of all I want to thank God and Google for allowing me to give my opinion regarding the professional services that I receive from this great firm, because to those who can read these short lines I can humbly suggest this great family of Lawyers, who are so professional and I am referring specifically to TELPNER PETERSON LAW FIRM, LLP. Well, by reviewing so much one by one I was able to read the stories and experience they had in a certain legal case, and I was able to see the Names of this great Firm TELPNER PETERSON, LAW, I was able to see that They helped obtain all the worker's rights in the event of an accident due to a work injury, so I contacted them immediately and the secretary who works at the reception answered, she very kindly answered my call and transferred the call to this great professional, I mean the Lawyer; RYAN J. MULDOO, attended to me in the same language, which is SPANISH, which I speak, we were able to arrange an appointment immediately because he was interested in my problem that I was going through, with this I want to tell you that what I liked the most is that he did not pay attention to my cultural range, or race, he immediately began to work on my case, which is my knee injury, to this day he continues to work hard, leaving all my concerns to him. concerns, because Attorney RYAN MULDOO is still very close to my Theraphy treatment, and the best thing is that he has brought the Insurance company up to date with my weekly payments, and has managed to get me good professional treatment with the best doctors so that I can receive the proper attention that a worker deserves, I encouraged all Hispanics and other nationalities to look for this good firm that is well represented, as is TELPNER PETERSON LAW FIRM, LLP. Given on July 11, 2017, in the city of New Orleans. Thank you so much.                            ATTE: EDGAR PLEITEZ.
Would highly recommend this firm and especially Chuck and Nicole for their knowledge and understanding and their years of experience.

My experience with this firm has been outstanding. Charles and Nicole and their staff have taken very good care of our case. They are very efficient and truly care about us as people, not just clients. I highly recommend this firm.

This firm is top notch.  They went above and beyond to help me and stayed in touch with me, usually via email ( my choice) with every little thing that happened during my case. I've been a member of the Omaha Bar Association for thirteen years.  The OBA has always fostered a close-knit community of attorneys in Omaha.  The organization constantly puts on great events that are well attended.  And the continuing education opportunities are endless, and keeping them in an on-demand library is super helpful.  Any attorney in Omaha would greatly benefit from becoming a member.
Being on disability with no money it’s very disappointing & disheartening that there are no pro bono or lost cost lawyers to help me.
reviews.ownerResponse: If you are looking for an attorney to help you pro bono or at a lower rate on a civil legal matter, we recommend contacting Legal Aid of Nebraska at 402-348-1069, or submit your legal question on the Nebraska State Bar Associations Free Legal Answers at https://ne.freelegalanswers.org/, or find resources and potential help at the Nebraska State Bar Association's Volunteer Lawyers Project at https://www.nevlp.org/. All 3 are great resources for members of the public who cannot afford an attorney.

reviews.text: Absolutely couldn't get any type of information or resources from all these Attorneys except for 1  law firm. Berry Law Office
reviews.ownerResponse: The Omaha Bar Association is a professional organization of attorneys. For those looking to speak with an attorney about a legal situation they are dealing with, we direct them to call our Lawyer Referral Service. We have links to many links to information and resources for members of the public looking for legal information. Feel free to call our LRS line at 402-280-3603.

reviews.text: Helping me file a complaint and investigation against 2 local attorneys.

I give Jon an easy 5-star rating for the help he provided to my 17 year-old son.  Jon explained the law to him carefully and clearly, as well as the implications of violating that law and potential outcomes affecting my son.  He also made it real clear to my son what the right response should have been.  He knows better now if ever in a similar situation.  Jon worked with the district attorney and handled all court appointments and requirements and resolved the case against my son in a very positive and satisfactory way.  I would highly recommend Jon to anyone asking.

My lawyer, Molly Burton,  was incredibly friendly, helpful, and knowledgeable throughout the entire process.  Her expertise and dedication truly made a difference, and I felt confident every step of the way. I highly recommend her to anyone seeking professional and reliable legal assistance!

He didn't protect the client and made a deal with the lawyer on the side that sued me that if I paid the other party's fees, the case would drop. He really just took things simple and wanted to take my money. I strongly advise against dealing with an unprofessional attorney on a single matter.

Great

Met with Chris Bartling, what a nice guy.  When you go to an attorney, regardless of the reason, you can be at a loss for what is needed. There are times you can feel like a deer on the headlights.  Chris made me feel very comfortable, walking through the issues and set me at ease.
Excellent Attorney's They Do Superior Work -I Highly  Recommend Them

Eric Hagen is an amazing attorney with an unrivaled attention to detail!

He was able to help us with an overly complicated case from over 30 years ago which included an identity theft that spanned multiple jurisdictions. He was transparent about the cost, the process, and knew exactly what needed to be done to get positive results.

Although we lived in a different state, Eric was in regular contact, making sure the paperwork was perfect before it was filed. Whenever we e-mailed him with questions or requesting an update, he was always quick to respond.

Once the hearing was scheduled, he called us multiple times to make sure we were able to travel, knew what to expect, and would come prepared. He checked that we arrived safely and was at our hotel first thing in the morning to discuss what was going to happen. He knew exactly what questions would be asked, what to focus our answers on, and was transparent about our chances of success.

We could tell that we made an excellent choice once the hearing began. His opening statement was so perfect, it left very few questions to answer. What we feared would be a long and complicated process was done within 5 minutes. We were outside celebrating before we could even register our success!

Looking back, we can’t imagine having to navigate this without Eric’s help.

Thank you, Eric, and God Bless you!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share this with us, Kathleen! We are so glad Eric was able to help you achieve the best possible outcome!

reviews.text: Seth Morris represented our family after we were involved in a vehicle accident. He did a tremendous job, and achieved a positive outcome for us. Would highly recommend.

Mike guided me through the difficult and often overwhelming process of divorce with clarity, professionalism, and genuine care. What felt like an impossible situation began to make sense with his help. Just a month later, my children are once again spending time with their father — something I truly feared might not happen.

I would not have reached this point without Mike and his incredible team. They worked tirelessly on my case, and it was clear from start to finish that they were fully committed to helping me and my family.

If you ever find yourself in a difficult situation with nowhere to turn, I highly recommend Mike Sands and his team. They are the real deal. Mike didn’t just handle my case — he helped bring my family back together.

reviews.text: I originally filed for Divorce in August of 2024 myself after that I met with Nathan for a consultation where he was very kind, understanding, and informational he gave me great advice for self representation at that time in 2024. I was drug through a living nightmare for about 14 months.  I then called Husker Law in September 2025 and Nathan spent an hour on the phone with me trying to get caught up with everything that went on with the Divorce Case in those 14 months. Nathan still took my case in October of 2025 despite all the obstacles he would have to clean up from the previous 14 months which included him representing me almost immediately on challenging the temporary child custody order. I obtained full legal soul custody in that hearing and Nathan, Brenda, and the staff at Husker Law walked right into my living nightmare with me they showed care and concern and supported me and did what they do best as a Law Firm. I signed my final divorce papers in December of 2025 in 3 months they cleaned up a 14 month living nightmare for me, I will forever be grateful for Nathan, Brenda, and the rest of the staff at Husker Law for helping me.  I have a very hard time trusting anyone Nathan had my trust in 2024 after the consultation he knew I wasn't going to be able to hire him at that time but he still did everything he could for me and he still took my case 14 months later without hesitation.

Unfortunately I wasn't well enough to attend the next court date either. Mike said he would take care of it.  Mike didn't get another continuance, he got my charges dropped completely.   Mike's years of work have helped him develop positive relationships with prosecutirs and judges. A lawyer without those relationships probably isn't able to get my charges dropped.
Throughout, Mike asked about my health and you could tell he genuinely cared.  If anyone needs criminal defense,, they can't do much better than Mike. James Watson has represented our family on several occasions. Not only is he an excellent lawyer, but he's a great human being. If you want thorough and ethical help, you are at the right place.

As of July 2022, Brett Ryan is under investigation by the Iowa Attorney Disciplinary Review Board for ethics violations. In my direct experience, he is a dishonest and unethical attorney.

Engage with him at your own risk.

UPDATE:

I cannot speak freely on this forum. When I started down this path, a different attorney cautioned the judge and legal process would 'run me out of the court room'. I was told that some lawyers/judges don't take kindly to a commoner playing in their sandbox. I moved forward because I believed it was the right thing to do. I moved forward to demonstrate how broken the property assessment appeal process is in Iowa.

A libel lawsuit was filed against me for the original review, which is posted above in it's entirety. I firmly believe the review contains verifiable facts or personal opinions from direct experience. We live in America; stating facts or expressing personal opinions is not libel. I did not settle any lawsuit. My insurance company, State Farm, decided it was cheaper to pay out $20K than pay to litigate the lawsuit and win. I can't control State Farm, but their choice has everything to do with minimizing overall cost to them and absolutely nothing to do with the merits of the case.

The lawyer in question represented Mills County (of which I am a taxpayer) in court due to property assessment litigation I initiated. I did not receive $1,500 in court sanctions. While suing my local government, the judge assessed a $100 sanction on me, along with court costs, for what amounted to a procedural violation.

At the end of the day, the public records from District Court, the Property Assessment Appeal Board, the Iowa Public Information Board, and the Attorney Disciplinary Review Board tell the story and I stand behind my efforts. I am a private citizen. I filed a property assessment appeal with my local government. The county hired a private attorney with taxpayer dollars to fight against me. My personal property tax liability has decreased dramatically over what it otherwise would have been. Unfortunately, the county didn't actually fix the core problem and as such, history is bound to repeat itself.

I am currently working with State Legislators to pass legislation to improve property tax equity for all Iowans, and hopefully, prevent local governments from hiring private attorneys to fight against citizens in property assessment appeals.

Laura and her team walked me through every aspect of what it means to take on a large employer, helped me navigate leaving my job and moving to a different state, and spent an entire DECADE supporting me, following through on every detail, and never ceasing the fervor at which they continued to pursue my case.  There was a very keen focus on doing what  was best for me, and continually treating me like a 'real human', not a case file.  There was a deep recognition that human beings deserve holistic  support when going through something that is so much more than a legal problem.  It's emotional, it's depressing, it's horrible, and Laura's team kept me from feeling overwhelmed and alone.  We've finally been able to reach a resolution with the other side, and I'm so grateful for Laura and her team for their kindess, respect, and diligence.  Highly recommend! 11/10!

This law firm and their lackeys at Clear Recovery garnished my minor daughter’s bank account for a debt of mine. My name, nor any of information is attached to her account. Cada tells me call Clear Recovery so I do, they tell me to call the county clerk. I also do that, county clerk tells me to call Cada. I do that again, then they tell me to call Clear Recovery. When I express frustration over all this run around, Cada becomes defensive and rude over the phone. The other very questionable part of this, I was served the papers at my place of employment, so why not just garnish my wages? Clearly know where I work and have worked at for years.  As far as I am concerned this is an all new predatory level of low for an $800 medical debt incurred by overpriced healthcare, WITH health insurance. Unreal the climate we live in with the law, healthcare and medical bills to take a minor’s paycheck from her first job because their parent couldn’t afford medical care. Ya’ll are as disgusting as our government. Shame.

I’ve been dealing with Cada law since 1966 and have always had the best results from Mr Cada going in today with my son who also is a client of Jim Cada, he is a very trusted attorney and gets things done right.
God Bless you Mr. Cada.

My experience with Cada, Cada, and Jewson, acting on behalf of a debt collector (PCR), was extremely disheartening. Rather than engaging in a good-faith effort to resolve the matter, they seemed solely interested in pushing the collector's agenda with no intention of settling. I spoke with Doug, who blatantly lied, claiming to have called me multiple times when, in reality, he hadn’t. It's absurd to think that such tactics can pass as professional conduct.

As an academic business professor, I find it deeply troubling that the current legal system allows debt collectors and their legal representatives to get away with questionable practices (they subpoenaed my mother-in-law, overall acted like a big bully), and in other states, such behavior would never be tolerated. It’s only a matter of time before this approach catches up with them. I ended up paying an extra $80 plus my debt since PCR is collecting court cost fees for the Zoom motion to trial I attended with Judge Reuter (Courtroom 21).

Unfortunately, I lost the focus to fight this battle, but I sincerely hope someone will have the energy to push this fight to the end and set a legal precedent. All in all, there's a lot of shady business going on to collect debt with no room to suggest the original firm (Gastroenterology Associates of Nebraska and Lincoln Digestive Health) did anything wrong. These two firms or entities are considered one at the time of practicing, but two when billing. Also, the firm gave a colonoscopy that should have been free but cost a fortune. It's crazy that a one minute change in the doctor's office's practice lead to this huge headache. PCR decided very early on that they were going to take me to court, probably because they saw my good credit. I hope you are stronger than me and can fight them to the end.

Filled out online form.  No answer after period of time.  Finally sent email saying it would be a good business practice if you would respond.  Jewson finally responded and said she would check on some things and get back to me.  Well that never happened.  Go figure!

They filed for a judgement against me in an entirely different County that had zero jurisdiction and brought my ex-wife into the matter who I've been divorced from for 7 years. Shady! These people are ethically immoral!
I’ve been represented by Cada law for over 55 years and have never had kinder, better service, they are the true honest caring lawyers, God bless you James (Jim) Cada)
