Scott has been a great asset for our family owned corporation.  He has been always accessible & very helpful.  His attention to detail has saved our corporation from sticky situations that could have been very damaging to our business.  We can always rely on his wisdom & experience.

We have been blessed to know attorney Scott Rogers of Peters Law Firm in Council Bluffs, Iowa, for more than 6 years.  His guidance and compassion have been immeasurably valuable to us.

Our parents were reasonably independent and living in their own house, thanks to  frequent visits, meal preparations, housework, errands, and facilitating trips to doctors by their daughter Jeannine who lived an hour away.  Mom had Alzheimer’s disease, so Dad did his best to handle her medications.  Our Dad was secretive about their financial affairs, so we had very limited knowledge about their powers of attorney, wills, investments, and preferences for potential future living arrangements.

Then at the end of 2014, Dad suffered his second broken hip from which he was never to fully recover.  What had been some cognitive and memory problems collapsed into obvious dementia.  Due to the severity of his physical and mental condition, and our Mom’s worsening Alzheimer’s disease, the doctors at the hospital would not release Dad until we had found a skilled nursing facility for both our parents.

It was then that we contacted our parents’ attorney, Scott Rogers.  Scott immediately stepped up to bat for us as we were thrust into the labyrinth of decisions and actions we needed to take on behalf of our parents.  He continually guided us through the process of making the best possible decisions for our parents, and through the actions needed to initiate those decisions.  He helped us locate and act on their durable powers of attorney, medical powers of attorney, wills, and trust documents.  He was always there for us as we decided on our parents’ future living arrangements, found the best skilled nursing care for them, pulled assets from their accounts to pay their bills, arranged for the sale of their house and cars, and prevented them from driving.  As our Dad and then our Mom eventually passed away, Scott provided critical advice and guidance in handling their affairs.  We also greatly appreciate Scott’s personal support, even to the extent of a funeral home visitation upon our Dad’s death.

Scott has been God sent.  Without him, we would not have known what questions to ask, let alone how to answer them.  His professional guidance, his personal compassion, and his overall support were far more than we expected.  In the strongest terms possible, we highly recommend attorney Scott Rogers.

John Bauman and Jeannine Christensen

Scott did not have to offer the added services that he did, but was thoughtful enough to do so. He was respectful of our wishes and answered all of our questions fully. Scott was very knowledgeable and helpful in handling our mom's estate. He went overeacb detail with us, never made us feel rushed or like our time didn't matter. It was a pleasure working with someone who helped make such a hard time in our lives less stressful. I'd recommend Scott to anyone who needs he's expertise.

Our corporation was an LDM client for several months. The attorney's bio says he "advises start-up corporations from seed to sale." Great, how about advising a senior executive of a start-up that 'paying huge legal retainers and investor dividends is typically reserved for fortune 500 companies?
Before a complicated merger of 4 companies into one (frequently insolvent) shell company happened, the LDM attorney was given due diligence on the senior executive orchestrating everything, that frankly, he should have performed himself. Instead of validating it -- heck, I invited him to invalidate it -- he was dismissve and chose to look the other way. As a result, for the last 12 months, the corporation has faced total chaos and has been and under finacial strain.
If the LDM attorney had performed DD, he would have found, among other things, the executive 'entrepreneur' had no track record of success in this industry and had mutiple dissolutions with other disgruntled partners of 5-6 other businesses.
* Dissatisfaction could not be reported to this firm during the 9 months it was retained because the executive, who hired this firm, had no qualms using other people's money to pay exorbitant legal fees to LDM and other law firms. He did not relinqusih his position with the corporation till after the LDM attorney resigned. The executive of our 'start-up' corporation spent over $90K+ in legal fees in 9 months dealing with disgruntled: contractors, employees, investors & acquisition owners (awesome for LDM Law Firm's accounts receivable dept.) The disgruntled-ness was due to the ineptness and lack of integrity of the executive who had a long history of "separations" from a dozen or more angry partners.The executive was, of cousre, pleased with the LDM attorney and thanked him often for having such a huge influence on the investors investing in his vision. These unsuspecting investors invested without doing any background checks on executive because they assumed the LDM attorney performed it ahead of time.

reviews.text:
Michelle Schlecht has represented me now on two separate occasions for related issues. David Copple assisted on the first issue. I could not have asked for better outcomes in either matter.

Here’s why you want Ms. Schlecht representing you:

1. She will manage your expectations. If you’re seeking counsel, more than likely you’ll be emotional about it. I know I have been. Both Ms. Schlecht and Mr. Copple told me what to expect - even though it wasn’t what I wanted to hear. However, in both cases, what unfolded was extremely close to what they initially suggested might transpire. They never made me any promises. But in the end, I was very pleased both times.

2. She comes prepared. In the two cases I experienced, she showed up better prepared than two different opposing counsels. It wasn’t even close.

3. She is organized. Again, I saw her better organized than two different opposing counsels. This may seem like a small detail, but I’ve seen its benefit.

4. She will promptly return your messages. I never expected an immediate response, but she ALWAYS returned a phone call or email within 24 hours.

If your matter ends up in the courtroom, the better prepared and more organized you are, the easier it is to react to the surprises that will more than likely follow you into the courtroom on the day of your hearing.

I highly recommend this firm, especially Ms. Schlecht and Mr. Copple. I’ve also heard great things about the other attorneys in this office from friends and family who have been represented by them.

Randy has been extremely amazing and has guided me through some of the toughest moments in my life. I thought my situation in the legal process was hopeless. I will recommend him to anyone seeking any sort of legal guidance.

Five Stars for Mike Winter – A True Advocate!

After dealing with a truly terrible lawyer (Larry Melcher) before finding Mike Winter (wish I had found him sooner), I was incredibly blessed to connect with him.

From the moment he stepped in — right as everything hit the fan — he walked straight into a legal swamp trap & handled it with strength, compassion & real dedication.

I’m facing what many agree is one of the most difficult & ugliest cases in Pottawattamie County history — a modern-day Kramer vs. Kramer (still can't believe I even remember that movie as a kid…), but Mike didn’t flinch & he’s still standing strong by my side.

He’s not just an excellent attorney — he’s the kind of person who won’t abandon you when things get tough.

In a legal world often full of darkness & confusion, Mike is the light. He listens. He fights. & most importantly, he believes.

B’ezrat Hashem & God willing, I expect — & hope — to win this case… even if “winning” simply means exposing her surreptitious absconding for what it truly was.

No matter the outcome, I know I’ve got the right man in my corner.

If you’re looking for an AMAZING COUNSELOR who truly cares & will go the distance for you, Mike Winter is your guy!

P.S.  The opposing party illegally qualified for Iowa Legal Aid—wielding taxpayer funds while we fought with grit (a modern-day David vs. Goliath).  Mike never flinched; he’s a warrior.

Despite thei opposing party & counsel unethical & dishonest behavior, truth & the one true God’s justice will prevail.

I’m plan to report Whitney Jacque to the Iowa State Bar for her misconduct.

I retained Mike twice over the last 20 some years To represent me in two divorces. I actually settled the first divorce in my deposition with my ex-wife. Which was impressed by, But in retrospect, I never got to see him work a divorce. 17 years go by. My third wife decided that she liked girls better…. But it was ugly. And when it got ugly, I begin to feel like Micheal and interrogating me more than representing me. After nearly 2 years of retainer (Which I will say was minuscule), But I was forced to hire a new council.

And then I found out from my new law firm that it only took 10 minutes on the telephone to get my soon to be ex-wife Attorney to agree that the prenup we signed was valid!!!

I love Mike. But he’s in his 70s and he likes playing poker and talking… If you need something done, I’m Not sure that he would be the guy to aggressively defend you. Forgive me, Mike. I just felt like I needed to tell the story.

I wish you all luck and whatever legal battle you have… But honestly, If I could go back, I would’ve heard my ex-wife Attorney!! Amie Galner Literally came at me so hard she almost got a judicial review!!!

I told Mike if I ever needed to hire somebody to tear someone’s you know what’s off…. I would hire her hands down!! She’s aggressive and vicious. And as her former opponent, I have to applaud her!!!

Even though she did trash my reputation as much as she could… But I’ve been my ex-wife. I would love to watch that as well.

Steve Delaney represented the defendant in my divorce. Delaney continuously missed court deadlines which dragged the trial date out longer and longer. One of those missed deadlines was producing discovery. The discovery he finally sent over was one document that was nearly 900 pages long.

In the final divorce decree, which Delaney drafted, he also requested to draft the QDROs, which are documents sent to retirement plan companies telling the company how to split retirement accounts.
It has been nearly 7 months since my divorce was finalized, and Delaney has yet to produce correct QDROs. He seems to ignore follow up requests from my attorney.

I estimate the amount of funds that I am to receive from retirement accounts is over $200,000. And it's being held hostage due to the fact that Steve Delaney will not complete his self-requested duties.

Divorce is already a terrible experience and much of this has felt like legal abuse.

I write this review with great disappointment and frustration. I was referred to this firm by my first attorney who had to withdraw from my case because of a conflict if interest. While the attorney who worked with me at Reagan, Melton & Delaney was very nice, I only had contact with her about 3 times.  Each time we went to court for a 20 minute pre-trial she asked for a continuance. Several months later, about a week before my trial was to begin, a separate attorney from this firm called and left me a message that my lawyer was on vacation so he needed to call me and tell me she was withdrawing from my case to become a judge. No one from the firm ever provided any direction for me after that.  Finally I went to another firm that expedited the process and I was exonerated of charges.  When I tried to claim my bond I was told the firm of Reagn, Melton & Delaney had taken it and I would have to go there to collect.  I went to their office ( for the first time as I had never been asked to come there before even though I was going to trial in a week) and they gave me my bond less $5000 for their fees.  I explained my case and that I thought the fee was excessive for the minor work they had done and the people up front (one was a lawyer and one was office staff) gave me a blank stare and continued to talk amongst themselves while completely ignoring me.  I feel taken advantage of, ignored, and completely disrespected in an already difficult time. To others reading this please do a considerable amount of research before hiring an attorney.

reviews.text:
I don’t even know where to begin. My case dates back to 2017. I was in a horrible hit-and-run accident that was not my fault. The kids who hit me had stolen the car and were on leave from juvenile detention. This case should have been simple and straightforward.

Instead, it dragged on for a full four years due to the constant turnover of paralegals at the firm. I was passed from one paralegal to another, which created confusion and delays. Mr. Inkelaar did absolutely nothing to help—he only got frustrated with me and spoke to me in a condescending manner. He made comments about me being a “young girl” and told me, “this is a lot of money,” as if I didn’t understand the value of my own case and health. He got into multiple heated arguments with both me and my father.

One of the paralegals once told me that my case should result in a substantial settlement due to the ongoing medical care I would need. When we brought this up to Mr. Inkelaar, he dismissed it and said she should have never told us that. It was clear he was not properly overseeing his employees—or my case.

I wanted to take the case to court, but he fought me on it. He was adamant about avoiding trial and completely disregarded my wishes. I felt like I was fighting against my own lawyer instead of having someone fighting for me.

To this day, I regret not switching lawyers. I wasn’t treated fairly, and I did not receive the compensation I deserved, especially considering how clear-cut the case should have been. I later met with two other attorneys to discuss my experience, and both told me it was absolutely absurd. They agreed I should have gone to court and likely would have received significantly more.

I regret this experience every single day. Absolutely do NOT go to this law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: We sincerely apologize for your experience and appreciate your feedback, as it is crucial for us to improve our services. It’s clear that we did not meet your expectations, and we regret that you felt unsupported throughout the process. Your concerns regarding communication and case management have been noted, and we take them seriously. We value each client's experience and want to ensure that all clients feel heard and advocated for. Thank you for bringing these issues to our attention, and we wish you the best moving forward.
Thank you so much for the 5-star rating—we truly appreciate your support!

reviews.text:
After a very long 3-year custody battle, I could not be more grateful for the representation and guidance I received from Greg. Today, I am walking away with full legal and physical custody of my child — something I prayed and fought for, and I truly could not have done it without him.

What I appreciated most was that he never just told me what I wanted to hear. He gave me realistic expectations at every stage, even when the truth was hard. He didn’t sugar coat things, but instead made sure I understood the process and was prepared for any outcome. That honesty built trust and helped me feel confident in every decision along the way.

When emotions were high, he stayed steady and focused. His professionalism, experience, and dedication were unmatched, but equally important, he genuinely cared about the outcome for my child and our future.

Today I cried happy tears knowing the court recognized what was best for my child. While our fight may not be completely over, I have absolute confidence that if and when challenges arise, Greg be prepared to face them head on.

I will forever be thankful for his hard work, patience, and unwavering commitment to my case. If you are looking for an attorney who will fight for you while also keeping you grounded in reality, I cannot recommend him highly enough.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your heartfelt experience, Megan. We're thrilled to hear about the positive outcome in your custody battle and that Greg's approach made such a meaningful impact on your journey. His commitment to providing realistic guidance while genuinely caring for clients and their families is a cornerstone of our practice. Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we wish you and your child the very best in this new chapter of your lives.

I had a truly excellent experience with Joseph D. Hall and Associates recently when I found myself in a distressing situation. I had fallen victim to an online scam and ended up losing my hard-earned $9,000 Hermes Birkin bag. However, thanks to the exceptional legal expertise and dedication of this team, I was able to successfully retrieve my prized possession.

From the very first moment I reached out to Jules, his professionalism and understanding of my situation were evident. He immediately grasped the gravity of what had happened and assured me he would do everything possible to rectify the situation.

He was not only well-versed in the complexities of online scams but also demonstrated a deep knowledge of intellectual property and brand protection. He meticulously studied the details of my case, gathered substantial evidence, and devised a robust legal strategy to approach the scammer.

What impressed me the most was the proactive approach. He kept me regularly updated on the progress of my case, ensuring that I was aware of every step he was taking to retrieve my Hermes Birkin bag. His clear communication and consistent availability to address my concerns alleviated much of the stress and anxiety that came with the situation.

Throughout the process, it was abundantly clear that Joseph D. Hall's team genuinely cared about securing a positive outcome for their clients. Their commitment to upholding justice was unwavering, and I TRULY felt like they had my best interests at heart.

When the day finally came and my $9,000 Hermes Birkin bag was recovered, I couldn't have been happier. They played a pivotal role in achieving this, and I am forever grateful for their expertise, dedication, and professionalism.

I wholeheartedly recommend this law firm to anyone seeking reliable and effective legal counsel. Rick Crowl is hands down the best lawyer I could have found. If I could give him more than 5 stars I would. Rick and his paralegal Kathy were amazing throughout my work comp case. It was on going for 5years. Rick and Kathy were on point, very consistent, and quick to respond the entire process. They were able to work with me when I was available so I wouldn't have to miss any work.
I chose Rick because I needed someone who had experience in multiple states and wasn't afraid to take a difficult case and get his hands a little dirty. Things were messy. I was overwhelmed and a year in when I realized the company, I was working for was only looking out for themselves. My company failed to info me of the Statute of Limitations. Rick was very honest and straightforward with me the entire time and made sure no deadlines were missed especially the Statute of Limitations which I knew nothing about until I got a personal lawyer.
Some advice from a person who "has been there done that" .....
#1 Get a personal lawyer NOT related to the company you work for from the start. (Rick Crowl)
#2 Don't give up. Be that squeaky wheel. Be organized.
#3 Take notes on absolutely everything. Write it all down include times/dates and what happened. You can't remember everything. The more you give your lawyer (Rick Crowl) the more he can help you.
#4 Get a physical copy from all appointments, all notes, and receipts for personal records. Keep everything together in a file box.
#5 No matter how many times a treatment clinic asks for your personal insurance/personal payment in case work comp doesn't pay don't do it. If it's work comp it's work comp. At every appointment state that it is work comp so there is no confusion.
#6 Let Rick Crowl at Stuart Tinley Law Firm make your process easier for you.

reviews.text:
This was my first time hiring an attorney and hopefully my last. George Jones and his team were by far the most unprofessional group I’ve ever dealt with.

I spoke with George in September 2024 about a clear case of surgical malpractice: my surgeon completely missed a fracture in the finger next to the thumb he operated on. At my follow-up, the surgeon admitted he missed it, and by the time he realized it wouldn’t heal, he said nothing could be done.

George agreed to look into it, and then nothing happened. For 11 months, his office dragged their feet. On July 31, I got an email saying they were still waiting on records from my physical therapist. The very next day, I received a letter saying they were dropping my case entirely even though they discovered in their findings that the surgeon failed to meet standards of care.

I am now left with permanent pain in my hand and will likely have to pay out of pocket for a future surgery.  Not to mention no time to find another attorney before the two-year statute of limitations runs out because it took them 11 months to review my medical records.

If you’re looking for someone who will fight for you, look elsewhere.  He doesn’t do the work anyways; he contracts other attorneys to do the work for him.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Fox, it is always my intention to speak directly with my clients when there is a decision to decline a case.  I will call you today to discuss your case, and I apologize that you didn't get a call from me before you received written notice.  Without getting into the specifics about your case, the review process in medical negligence cases is rarely quick.  We generally need to allow the client to complete treatment or reach a plateau so we can determine what damages there will be in the future.  Then we gather the records and submit them to a paid expert for review.  Despite often spending thousands of dollars on records and medical experts, we are able to move forward with only a small percentage of cases after we have done our review.  We need our expert to be able to give an opinion that there was an act of negligence, and that the act of negligence caused damages.  Please allow me to review your records today and give you a call to discuss the details of our decision in your case.  I truly appreciate the opportunity you gave us to review your case and will do my best to answer your questions.

reviews.text: I had a very difficult realty and bad debt problem that I tried to resolve with numerous legal pleas by myself for three years, only to be frustrated at every step. His professionalism, his courtesy, and his expertly written plea were of the highest caliber.  George is a brilliant gentleman, a superb attorney, and a fond friend.

Eric Hagen is an amazing attorney with an unrivaled attention to detail!

He was able to help us with an overly complicated case from over 30 years ago which included an identity theft that spanned multiple jurisdictions. He was transparent about the cost, the process, and knew exactly what needed to be done to get positive results.

Although we lived in a different state, Eric was in regular contact, making sure the paperwork was perfect before it was filed. Whenever we e-mailed him with questions or requesting an update, he was always quick to respond.

Once the hearing was scheduled, he called us multiple times to make sure we were able to travel, knew what to expect, and would come prepared. He checked that we arrived safely and was at our hotel first thing in the morning to discuss what was going to happen. He knew exactly what questions would be asked, what to focus our answers on, and was transparent about our chances of success.

We could tell that we made an excellent choice once the hearing began. His opening statement was so perfect, it left very few questions to answer. What we feared would be a long and complicated process was done within 5 minutes. We were outside celebrating before we could even register our success!

Looking back, we can’t imagine having to navigate this without Eric’s help.

Thank you, Eric, and God Bless you!

I'm willing to bet if they are doing this to me, it's happening elsewhere, to others. Banks must be stopped, It's unscrupulous. It's the principle, and if Dan isn't  interested in looking into unscruplous practices, which others may also be experiencing, what good is the law?

Long story short, I heard about Peters Law Firm from a professional business friend of mine here in Shenandoah.

I contacted a high school classmate of my brother, who also works at the same law firm, and he passed me on to Jordan. Jordan has experience in work comp law.

I met with Jordan and he looked over my file of all the paperwork associated with the work comp case. He didn’t have to study it very long, maybe less than 15 minutes, before Jordan said he’d take the case.

When the case went to hearing, we asked for a 50% industrial disability rating. The administrative law judge, after more than a year after the hearing, awarded us 45%. I was pleased.

I highly recommend Jordan.  He’s professional, young, a family man and honest.   I would hire him again!!

reviews.text: I want to share what an amazing experience it was for my significant other working with Mallory Hughes and Ted Turnblacer. We hired Mallory over a year ago to handle their case after they were falsely accused (case closed 11/2025), and from the very beginning, her professionalism, knowledge, and dedication were incredible. Mallory and Ted handled the case with precision and clarity, making an extremely stressful situation much more manageable. I wasn’t directly involved in the evidence or day-to-day process, but Mallory kept my significant other informed and supported every step of the way. Their attention to detail and strategic thinking gave us hope when it felt like there wasn’t any. What really stood out was how committed they were to getting the best outcome. They’re persistent, skilled, and effective advocates, and they genuinely care about the people they represent. Because of their hard work and guidance, my significant other was found not guilty, and I can’t express how grateful we are. For anyone reading this and looking for hope, I want to say: it’s possible. At first, it felt like there was no way this could work out. Our lives were completely put on hold. we made sacrifices in our careers, plans, and daily routines. There were tears, arguments, and really stressful moments, but we never stopped trusting God and praying, while knowing my significant other had the best team in their corner. This experience showed me that you can never fully predict the outcome, but when it’s positive, it’s life-changing. If you’re looking for someone who is knowledgeable, compassionate, and truly fights for their clients, I would highly recommend Mallory Hughes and Ted Turnblacer.

I was a student studying abroad by myself. I had a first car accident ever in my life, and I didn't know what to do at all. Because my parents were back in my home country, I had no one to help.

I didn't have a phone, but it was a busy time of the semester, so I couldn't keep asking my friend to borrow her phone. I tried to find someone who can communicate with me only through email.

I found this company and got help from Ms. Steinkemper. Because I had lots of exams going on and had to stay up late every night, I didn't have time to take care of the car accident. Thankfully, Ms. Steinkemper took care of all the documents needed, and whenever I had questions about the accident, she replied to me on time.

What I had to do was explaining the situation to her, sending the bills, and signing a contract. Thanks to her, I could entirely focus on my academics and finish my semester without any problem.

Also, I was able to get more than twice the compensation that the driver's insurance company suggested to me when I contacted them directly. I think she spoke for me well and got a reasonable compensation for me.

I was really satisfied with this company, and I've been highly recommending the company to other students, especially international students whose parents are not here to help.

Thank you again, Ms. Steinkemper, for reaching me out for help.
I was one of the worst experience ever for me to have an accident, but I could go through it with your help.
